candidates.name: Abigail Spanberger
candidates.notes:
Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (75th Gov, first woman); won 2025 race ~57% over Lt. Gov. Earle-Sears. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Progressive on social/cultural issues with centrist-Dem posture on policing and a Blue Dog fiscal record. Heritage Action 5%. Pro-abortion: cosponsored + voted YES (twice) on the federal Women's Health Protection Act; signed SB449 Feb 6 2026 sending VA reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment to Nov 2026 ballot; signed contraceptive-access bills April 2026; endorsed by Planned Parenthood, EMILYs List, Reproductive Freedom for All. Pro-same-sex-marriage: voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act 2022 (twice); signed HB612 marriage-amendment ballot referral; original cosponsor Equality Act; HRC endorsement. Anti-school-choice: campaign platform 'oppose efforts that take public dollars out of public schools'; 'get culture wars out of our schools.' Pro-gun-control: cosponsored + voted YES on Assault Weapons Ban 2022 (HR1808); signed VA SB749/HB217 assault-weapons ban May 14–15 2026. Pro-mass-mail-in voting / opposes SAVE Act citizenship verification. Anti-sanctuary-cooperation: Executive Directive 1 (Feb 4 2026) terminated all VA 287(g) agreements (State Police, DOC, Wildlife Resources, Marine Resources); voted NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act 2023. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: famous Nov 2020 caucus-call quote 'No one should say defund the police ever again'; co-led bipartisan Invest to Protect Act for police funding (PolitiFact rated false the GOP claim she backs defunding); Iron Dome funding YES; bipartisan Social Security Fairness Act signed into law.

[2026-07-18 VA sprint] Web re-verified sitting Governor: sworn Jan 17, 2026 (Ballotpedia Governor of Virginia). 2025 general: Spanberger (D) 57.6% / 1,976,857 vs Earle-Sears (R) 42.2% / 1,449,586 (certified VA results via Ballotpedia + ELECT ENR). Website corrected from stale house.gov URL to governor.virginia.gov. Dens remains partial (21/49); no score changes this pass.

[2026-07-18 sprint] HARD: Signed Right to Contraception Act; June 2026 Dobbs anniversary statement defending reproductive rights (governor.virginia.gov). HARD: Signed bill placing Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment on Nov 3 2026 ballot (Ballotpedia; Planned Parenthood Action 2026-02-06). HARD (prior House): NAY Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act 2023 (clerk.house.gov/Votes/2023192) per Virginia Mercury 2025-09-09. SOFT: Mercury — would veto further abortion restrictions; opposes Youngkin bathroom/pronoun school policies; LGBTQ student rights.

profile.education: University of Virginia (BA French Literature), Purdue/GISMA Business School (MBA)
profile.background: 75th Governor of Virginia (first woman); sworn in Jan 17, 2026. Former US Rep VA-07 (2019–2025, 3 terms; did not seek reelection 2024). Prior career: CIA case officer (counterterrorism); US Postal Inspector. Blue Dog Coalition + New Democrat + Problem Solvers Caucus member in the House.

china.note: $50,000 from Pin Ni (CCP member, Wanxiang) in two $25,000 installments April + May 2025, supporting Virginia gubernatorial bid. Pin is named in Chinese state media 15 times as 'outstanding Communist Party member.'
china.descriptor: China-linked donor tracking. The CCP operates an explicit United Front Work Department to direct overseas members and proxies — including business executives such as Wanxiang Group's Pin Ni (named CCP member 15 times in Chinese state media) — to cultivate political relationships in the United States. Federal law prohibits CCP members from donating to U.S. campaigns, and most CCP-member donors hold green cards rather than citizenship, raising significant legal questions. RESOLUTE Citizen treats any documented contribution from a CCP-member or United Front-affiliated donor as a failure on america_first/q3.

candidates.name: Ghazala Hashmi
candidates.notes:
Lt. Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026; first Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Prior: VA Senate SD-15 (Richmond/Chesterfield) 2020-2026, Education & Health Chair. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Solidly progressive across the rubric. Patron of SJ247 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment) AND SJ249 (2025 same-sex-marriage amendment). Sponsored SB280 (2024 'Death with Dignity' physician-assisted suicide; passed Senate 21-19) and SB1098 (2025 shield law for out-of-state abortion + gender-medicine providers). As Ed/Health Chair, panel killed 2023 parental-rights bills on school books and student gender-identity notification, and rejected charter-school expansion. Voted for Virginia Values Act 2020 (state SOGI nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out). Patron of SB5035 (2020 spec session) civilian police-oversight boards with binding disciplinary authority including termination. Sponsored 'Cover All Kids' Medicaid-like coverage for undocumented children. Voted YES on SB1 (2025) semi-auto firearms + high-capacity-mag ban (Youngkin-vetoed). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, Reproductive Freedom for All, ReproRising VA PAC, Giffords, Everytown ('gun sense champion'), Brady, VA LGBT+ Democrats. June 2026 (as LG) publicly attacked Commonwealth's Attorneys who declined to enforce the new assault-weapons ban. No cross-party deviations found.

[2026-07-18 VA sprint] Web re-verified sitting Lt. Governor (sworn Jan 17, 2026). 2025 general: Hashmi (D) 55.7% / 1,900,104 vs John Reid (R) 44.1% / 1,505,395 (Ballotpedia + ELECT ENR). Dens 17/49 — continue state evidence pass next.

candidates.name: Jay Jones
candidates.notes:
Attorney General of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (49th AG, first Black AG); won 2025 race 53-46 vs R incumbent Miyares. Prior: VA House Delegate HD-89 (Norfolk) 2018-2022. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Reliably progressive record on the marquee cells: self-confirmed past board member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood'; co-patron 2019 'Repeal Act' (HB2491) removing ultrasound + 24-hour-wait abortion restrictions; PP Advocates VA + Reproductive Freedom For All + ReproRising VA PAC + Roe Your Vote endorsements; Aug 2025 hosted 'Reproductive Freedom Roundtable' with PP. Voted YES on Virginia Values Act 2020 (LGBTQ nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out); VA LGBT+ Democrats endorsement. Co-sponsored/voted YES on 2020 Dem omnibus gun package; Everytown $200K + endorsement (later scrubbed after texts surfaced); Giffords + Brady + Prevent Gun Tragedies PAC endorsements; as AG-elect Dec 2025 intervened to salvage VA's universal background-check law on appeal. Anti-sanctuary-ban: 2018 voted NO on HB1257 (would have banned sanctuary jurisdictions); Day-1 as AG announced review of Miyares opinion authorizing ICE detainers. CRITICAL EVIDENCE — 2022 text scandal: Aug 2022 text exchange with R Del. Carrie Coyner surfaced October 2025 (National Review): Jones wrote of then-Speaker Todd Gilbert 'Three people, two bullets / Gilbert, hitler, and pol pot / Gilbert gets two bullets to the head' and that Gilbert's children were 'breeding little fascists' he hoped would die because 'Only when people feel pain personally do they move on policy,' and that he'd 'go to their funerals to piss on their graves.' Coyner replied: 'Please stop … It really bothers me when you talk about hurting people or wishing death on them.' Jones publicly apologized ('reading back those words made me sick to my stomach'). Coyner separately alleges a 2020 phone call where Jones said about police 'Well, maybe if a few of them died, that they would move on, not shooting people, not killing people' — contested by Jones. Spanberger called the comments 'disgusting.' Jones was co-patron of 2020 qualified-immunity ban with floor quote 'take a whole bunch of frivolous lawsuits if it means that someone who is shot 7 times in the back gets a day in court.'

[2026-07-18 VA sprint] HARD roster: sworn AG Jan 17, 2026 (oag.state.va.us). 2025 result 53.1%–46.5% over Jason Miyares (Ballotpedia). HARD self-statement: Virginia Planned Parenthood board + abortion-rights advocacy (official AG about page + campaign about). conf upgraded evidence_curated→evidence_state. Dens still only 12/49 — more AG-era evidence needed; no reset this pass.

claims.text: Plans to join multistate coalitions pushing back against state and federal abortion restrictions, and supports Virginia's ongoing constitutional-amendment process to enshrine abortion protections in the state constitution — explicitly opposing any life-at-conception or fetal-personhood standard the rubric upholds.

Helped pass Virginia's red-flag law as a member of the House of Delegates; upon election as attorney general-elect, immediately sought a court extension of Virginia's universal background-check gun laws after a circuit court ruled them partially unconstitutional — actively defending the red-flag and universal-background-check mechanisms the rubric explicitly opposes as infringements on Second Amendment rights.

Stated that 'immigration enforcement is the job of the federal government' and that local law enforcement 'already has a massive job keeping communities safe from violent crime' — a pro-sanctuary posture opposing local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement that directly contradicts the rubric's anti-sanctuary standard.

claims.text: Voted NO on Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment (2026), which would have embedded a broad, near-unlimited abortion right into the Virginia Constitution. Virginia Choice Tracker confirms French as 'Anti-Choice' for this vote. The amendment passed the legislature (Senate 21-19 on strict party lines) and is on track for a November 2026 voter referendum — with French casting one of 19 Republican 'no' votes in the Senate.

Voted against the Virginia Reproductive and Gender-Affirming Health Care Act, which would have protected the right to provide or receive gender-affirming care — including puberty blockers and cross-sex hormone treatments — under state law, shielding transgender medical ideology from any future legislative correction. Virginia Choice Tracker documents French's no vote as part of a consistent pattern of rejecting gender-ideology mandates.

Voted against the 2026 Virginia gun-control package including the assault-weapons ban (SB 749), joining every Republican senator in opposing legislation the NRA-ILA called unconstitutional. The bill passed 21-19 on party lines; French cast one of the 19 'no' votes defending Virginians' Second Amendment rights against the most sweeping firearms restriction in Virginia's modern legislative history.

candidates.name: Mark Obenshain
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-2 (R), since 2004 — deep voting record. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Reliable conservative across the board: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (born-alive floor quote; ChoiceTracker 'anti-choice'); NO on the same-sex-marriage amendment (not a crossover); YES on the 2016 religious-freedom bill (SB41); longtime school-choice/ESA sponsor; architect of VA photo voter-ID + SAVE-program citizenship-verification bills; tough-on-crime (bail-presumption bill, opposed qualified-immunity repeal); opposed 2026 ICE-restriction bills; opposed the 2020 red-flag law. Note: do NOT confuse with Del. Chris Obenshain (House), a relative who crossed over on marriage.

claims.text: Co-sponsored Virginia's most protective pro-life legislation introduced in the 2023 General Assembly session: a bill by Sen. Travis Hackworth (with Obenshain and Newman co-patroning) to limit abortion from conception with narrow medical-emergency, rape, and incest exceptions. Though defeated in committee by a Democratic majority, Obenshain's co-sponsorship placed him among the senators most committed to protecting unborn life from the moment of fertilization.

Voted against the 2026 Virginia assault-weapons ban (SB 749) and broader gun-control package. Publicly called the AWB 'really one of the more extreme bills that is gonna pass this year' and separately blasted a gun-storage mandate bill as unconstitutional: 'It would criminalize most modern pistols and would not last five minutes under any constitutional scrutiny.' He also assembled and circulated a video montage of Democrats admitting the bills would not stop criminals — highlighting the legislation's futility in keeping the public safe.

Publicly opposed SB1031 (2025 session), a Democratic bill that would have stripped Virginia's religious homeschooling exemption and imposed new regulatory requirements on families who educate at home for faith-based reasons. Obenshain stated: 'I have long believed that one size fits all education does not work for every family in Virginia' and 'I believe parents ought to have the right to make decisions concerning their child' — calling the bill 'an ill-advised attack on parents who choose to homeschool their children.'

claims.text: A Baptist deacon (Bonsack Baptist Church, Roanoke) and small-business owner who has described himself as a 'stalwart defender of the unborn' throughout his 12 years in the Virginia House of Delegates (2012-2024) and continuing in the State Senate; consistently votes against abortion-expanding legislation and opposed Virginia's reproductive freedom constitutional amendments.

Publicly identifies as 'pro-second amendment' and has consistently voted against gun-control measures introduced by Democrats in the Virginia General Assembly; cited Second Amendment protection as a core legislative priority when campaigning for the Senate seat in 2023.

As a member of the Virginia Senate Education and Health Committee, Head has consistently backed parental rights legislation in the Commonwealth. During the 2024 session, the committee considered SB 37 ('Sage's Law'), which would have required school principals to notify parents if a student expressed gender incongruence and to obtain parental permission before implementing any school-based gender transition plan. Head, as a Republican committee member, voted in the minority in favor of the bill when the Democrat majority voted 9-6 to kill it. His continued committee service in the 2025-2026 sessions reflects ongoing advocacy for parental rights in public education.

Sponsored legislation to establish uniform statewide firearms ordinances by clarifying their applicability to properties located across multiple localities — a measure aimed at preventing local jurisdictions from creating a patchwork of contradictory gun ordinances that create legal traps for law-abiding concealed-carry permit holders traveling across jurisdiction lines. This statewide preemption approach strengthens consistent carry rights for Virginians and aligns with constitutional carry principles by subordinating local gun restrictions to uniform state standards.

claims.text: Suetterlein supported in committee a bill that would have banned abortion in Virginia completely — with only limited exceptions — and imposed felony penalties for performing illegal abortions. He has repeatedly stated he views abortion as 'a tragedy' and has advocated for increasing contraceptive access and family life education to reduce abortion demand, while opposing the broader availability of elective abortion.

Voted NO on SJ247 (January 21, 2025), the constitutional amendment to embed an absolute 'right to reproductive freedom' in the Virginia constitution — a measure that would prohibit any state regulation of abortion at any stage of pregnancy. Suetterlein was among all 19 Senate Republicans who rejected the amendment.

Voted NO on SB749 (2026), Virginia's assault-firearms and high-capacity magazine ban. Suetterlein has a track record of passing more than 50 bills into law, many with bipartisan support, but drew a clear line against gun restrictions, opposing the Democrat-backed firearms ban on constitutional grounds alongside all Senate Republicans.

claims.text: Voted NO on Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment (2026) — a proposed constitutional amendment that would have enshrined a sweeping right to abortion, including post-viability procedures with broad health exceptions, in the Virginia Constitution. The measure passed the Senate 21-19 on a strict party-line vote; Stanley's 'no' vote aligned with every Republican senator in defense of the unborn.

Voted against the 2026 Virginia assault-weapons ban (SB 749) and the broader gun-control package, standing with every Republican senator in opposing legislation the NRA-ILA called 'unconstitutional.' The package — the largest gun-restriction measure in Virginia history — banned the sale and manufacture of assault-style firearms, restricted magazine capacity, and imposed new storage mandates. Gov. Spanberger signed it into law on May 15, 2026; NRA and Virginia gun organizations immediately filed suit.

Authored and championed Virginia SB 1515 (2023), requiring any commercial website where at least one-third of its content is pornographic to verify users are 18 or older via government ID, biometric scan, or approved software before granting access. Governor Youngkin signed the bill; it took effect July 1, 2023. Stanley stated his purpose was to protect children: '30% of 10-year-olds have access [to online pornography], and by age 13 that number jumps to 85%.' The bill received near-unanimous bipartisan support in the General Assembly — a direct government check on the commercial promotion of sexual content to minors.

Stanley ran in his 2011 special-election campaign on an unambiguous fiscal-discipline pledge: 'I will never vote to raise your taxes or user fees at any time' and 'I will fight to reduce state spending and the size of state government.' His 2025 legislative session bills reflect those commitments: SB 61 (lower corporate income tax rate for certain businesses), SB 68 (industrial building rehabilitation tax credit), and SB 70 (a two-year retail sales and use tax moratorium on construction materials and supplies) — all oriented toward reducing the tax burden on Virginia businesses and communities.

candidates.name: Creigh Deeds
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-11 (sitting since 2001). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long-tenured mainstream Senate Democrat. Pro-abortion-rights HARD record: named 2023 'Reproductive Freedom Champion' by ReproRising (formerly NARAL), endorsed by the group, in the Democratic majority advancing the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1). Marriage: in the majority advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (2025 & 2026 references) and publicly opposed Youngkin's transgender-student model policy. Opposed school choice/ESAs ('nonstarter… defund public schools') and voter ID (urged a 2012 veto). Economic: backs the $15 minimum wage. Public justice: sponsored bail reform (SB1266) and voted to abolish the death penalty (2021). Guns: sponsored an assault-weapons ban (SB1382), backed universal background checks/safe-storage; GIFFORDS-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT (historical, noted not scored): in 2009 voted against in-state tuition/benefits for undocumented immigrants and for English-as-official-language — restrictionist votes atypical of his caucus, but 2009-era and not mapping to the current border cells. Several amendment roll-call lines are party-line-majority inferences (VPAP blocked automated read) but his alignment is independently corroborated by HARD endorsements/statements. No conservative deviations on the scored cells.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (member, Presbyterian Church)
profile.background: VA State Senator since 2001 (House of Delegates 1992-2001); attorney, former Bath County Commonwealth's Attorney; 2005 AG nominee and 2009 Democratic gubernatorial nominee. SD-11 is a sprawling central/western VA district (Bath, Alleghany, Charlottesville/Albemarle area) after redistricting from the old SD-25. Virginia's leading mental-health-reform legislator following his son's 2013 death.
profile.education: Concord College (BA, 1980), Wake Forest University School of Law (JD, 1984)

claims.text: Following the 2022 University of Virginia campus shooting, Deeds introduced SB 272 in successive sessions (2023, 2024, 2025, and 2026) to prohibit firearms in state-owned buildings including public university campuses, narrowing carry exemptions to only approved institutional activities; the bill has passed both chambers each session and is awaiting final action in 2026, representing an ongoing effort to restrict lawful carry rights.

Deeds introduced legislation in the Virginia Senate to make it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any civilian to import, sell, manufacture, purchase, possess, transport, or transfer an 'assault firearm' — one of the most sweeping anti-gun proposals in the Virginia legislature — reflecting his consistent record of opposing constitutional carry and civilian firearm ownership.

candidates.name: Glen Sturtevant
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-12 (returned to the Senate 2024; prior service 2016-2020). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent Christian-conservative record. Life: self-described '100% pro-life,' graded F by ReproRising 2025, opposed the reproductive-freedom amendment with the GOP bloc. Marriage (VERIFIED, the key cell): voted NO on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) — the ONLY 5 Republican crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, and Suetterlein; Sturtevant was in the 13-member NO bloc, did NOT cross. Family: backs school choice ('money follows the child') and sponsored parental content-opt-out legislation. Elections: supports restoring photo voter ID. Immigration: restrictionist; sponsored an ICE-transfer bill for incarcerated persons. Guns: opposed the 2026 mandatory safe-storage bill and argued to repeal the red-flag law. ONE DOCUMENTED RECORD DEVIATION (noted, not scored): voted FOR red-flag legislation in 2019 but now favors repeal — his current position is opposition, which is what's scored. Cells with no public evidence (christian liberty, federal overreach, CBDC/ESG, death-penalty, a numeric Giffords grade) are left null, NOT scored from party.

claims.text: Introduced SB548 (2025 session), which would have required that born-alive infants — babies who survive an attempted abortion — receive the same medical treatment and care as any other live-born child, with criminal penalties for non-compliance. The Democrat-controlled Senate killed the bill in committee. Sturtevant publicly declared the Democrats' competing abortion constitutional amendment 'the most extreme abortion amendment in the nation' because it bars the state from regulating abortion in any way.

Voted NO on SB749 (2026), the Democrat-sponsored assault-firearms ban banning sale and purchase of 'assault firearms' and magazines over 15 rounds. Sturtevant joined all 18 other Senate Republicans in opposing the bill; the NRA and Virginia Citizens Defense League (VCDL) both opposed the measure as unconstitutional.

Voted NO on SJ247 (January 2025), the constitutional amendment to embed an unlimited 'right to reproductive freedom' in Virginia's constitution with no exceptions for gestational limits, parental consent, or health regulations. Sturtevant warned the amendment 'would protect' human traffickers and adults who impregnate minors by preventing prosecution if the victim obtains an abortion in Virginia.

claims.text: Co-sponsored SJ247 (introduced November 2024, voted January 21, 2025), Virginia's constitutional amendment establishing a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' that explicitly prohibits the state from regulating abortion in any way that burdens that right. Aird was among the Democratic co-sponsors who moved the amendment through the Senate 21-19 on a party-line vote. The ACLU of Virginia called the passage 'historic'; SBA Pro-Life America and Virginia's pro-life groups strongly opposed it.

Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia in her 2023 state Senate primary — a rare intervention by the organization in a contested Democratic primary. Aird's campaign also received financial and organizational support from Planned Parenthood's Virginia affiliate, whose endorsement was centered on her commitment to expanding abortion access. The rubric requires legislators to have never accepted money or in-kind support from Planned Parenthood, NARAL, or EMILY's List.

Voted YES on SB749 (2026 session), Virginia's assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, importation, manufacture, or transfer of semi-automatic centerfire rifles and magazines over 15 rounds. The Senate passed the bill 21-19, with Aird joining all 20 other Democratic senators in favor. The Virginia Citizens Defense League rated every Democratic senator who voted YES a 'F' on Second Amendment fidelity for the 2026 session.

claims.text: Voted YES on SJ247 (January 21, 2025), the Virginia constitutional amendment to create a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' — defined to include abortion care with no gestational limit — with all 21 Democratic senators voting in favor 21-19 over united Republican opposition. As Chair of the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus and Chair of the Democratic Party of Virginia, Bagby was a key voice in advancing the progressive agenda that included this reproductive freedom amendment.

Voted YES on the 2025 Virginia constitutional amendment (first passage) to repeal Article I, Section 15-A — the 2006 voter-approved provision defining marriage in Virginia as solely between one man and one woman. The repeal measure passed the Senate 21-18 on January 21, 2025, with Bagby and his Democratic colleagues providing the decisive margin. The amendment received its required second passage in 2026 and was placed on the November 2026 statewide ballot.

Voted YES on SB749 (2026), Virginia's assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, and transfer of semi-automatic centerfire rifles with detachable magazines and certain features, plus magazines over 15 rounds. The Senate passed the bill 21-19 on a party-line vote; Bagby joined all Democratic senators in voting in favor. Governor Spanberger signed the bill in May 2026.

candidates.name: Michael Jones
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-15 (sitting since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77; former Richmond City Council President). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] HONEST KEY FINDING: Jones is a Christian pastor (Village of Faith Ministries), but his LIS-verified recorded votes are uniformly progressive — pastoral identity did NOT produce a socially conservative voting record, and he is scored on his votes, not his vocation. Life: co-patron + YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HB781/SB449 2026); ReproRising A+. Marriage: YES on HJ9 (2025) repealing the one-man/one-woman definition and YES on HB612/SB311 (2026). Economic: YES on multiple minimum-wage bills and paid family leave. Elections: YES on the felon-voting-rights amendment (HJ2) and on expanding in-person absentee voting/registration. Public justice: introduced bills banning tear gas/rubber bullets/chokeholds and voted for marijuana-offense resentencing. Guns: chief patron of an auto-sears ban (HB22) and YES on the assault-firearms/high-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607); GIFFORDS-endorsed. Across ~2,094 recorded 2025 House votes he cast only 3 idiosyncratic NO votes, none a conservative break. christian_liberty (a church-property tax-exemption YES does not map to the symbol/prayer/conscience cells), refuse_federal_overreach, school choice, and sanctuary cells left null.

profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-15 (parts of Richmond City and Chesterfield County) since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77 (2024-2026) and Richmond City Council member/President (2016-2023). claims.text: As a Virginia Delegate in 2024, Jones sponsored House Bill 22 banning the manufacture, importation, sale, possession, or transfer of auto sears — devices that convert semi-automatic firearms to fully automatic fire — making violations a Class 6 felony effective July 1, 2024. The bill represents an incremental expansion of state restrictions on legal firearm components, contrary to the rubric's defense of unrestricted Second Amendment rights.

Co-sponsored the Virginia constitutional amendment (SJ247/2025) to enshrine a broad 'right to reproductive freedom' in the state constitution — explicitly rejecting any legislative recognition of personhood from conception. The amendment, which Jones helped advance through the House of Delegates, would prohibit the Commonwealth from regulating abortion in any way that burdens that right.

Co-sponsored the Virginia constitutional amendment (2025 session) to enshrine equal marriage rights for same-sex couples in the state constitution, codifying a legal definition of marriage that rejects the one-man-one-woman standard the rubric requires. Jones has also authored legislation extending insurance benefits to same-sex and unmarried cohabiting couples.

candidates.name: Schuyler VanValkenburg
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico); sitting since Jan 2024; term runs through 2028. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Co-patron + YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 2025 / HJ1 2026); ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'); ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice' (Planned Parenthood Action endorsed). YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026). Vocal opponent of Education Savings Accounts / school choice. Lead sponsor of legislation returning VA to the ERIC multistate voter-data partnership; in the Democratic majority that advanced felon-voting restoration (HJ2). In the Senate Democratic bloc that passed the 2026 ICE-cooperation restrictions (21-19 party line) and the $15 minimum-wage law; championed the 2026 gun-reform package (assault-weapons ban, magazine limit, red-flag expansion). claims.text: Schuyler VanValkenburg co-sponsored the Repeal Act — a Virginia bill to eliminate existing restrictions on abortion later in pregnancy — and has consistently backed legislation protecting abortion access through all trimesters under medical discretion, rejecting any life-at-conception or personhood standard. He supports Virginia's constitutional amendment to enshrine the right to reproductive freedom in the state constitution.

VanValkenburg introduced legislation creating felony charges for parents or guardians who allow a child under 18 to access a firearm — the Lucia Bremer Act (named for a 13-year-old shot by a classmate). He declared safe gun-storage legislation would be his first bill in the Senate, stating 'We can and must do more to keep our kids safe from gun violence.' He opposes constitutional-carry and unrestricted access principles the rubric supports.

candidates.name: Emily Jordan
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024 (former Delegate HD-64). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record (Family Foundation report card 95% pro-family, 18 of 19 votes) with ONE notable deviation. KEY DEVIATION (verified, LIS roll call): voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. On the same day she held the conservative line on the other two amendments: NO on HJ1 (abortion) and NO on HJ2 (felon voting), so the marriage vote is a deliberate, isolated break, not a moderate drift. Pro-life voting (NO HJ1); pro-parental-notification (NO on SB19/HB1499 narrowing the explicit-material notice law; YES SB817; YES SB778 obscene-material-to-minor penalty); YES HB131 (religious accommodations in higher ed); fiscal-conservative (NO on the $15 minimum wage SB1); election-integrity (NO on expanded Sunday absentee SB438, NO on felon restoration); pro-police (NO on SB352 restricting LEO, YES SB55/SB111 penalties, NO on SB60 parole expansion); anti-sanctuary (NO on SB783/HB1441 ICE-restriction bills); strongly pro-2A (NO on assault-weapons ban SB749, red-flag SB495, storage mandate SB348).

candidates.name: L. Louise Lucas
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-18; Senate President pro tempore; Chair, Finance & Appropriations; in the GA since 1992. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive and unusually activist HARD record: CHIEF PATRON of all three 2026 constitutional amendments — reproductive freedom (SJR1), same-sex marriage (SJR3, repealing the Marshall-Newman ban), and felon-voting restoration (SJR2). ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'). VCDL 2025 record 0% pro-gun; chief patron of the 2026 assault-weapons ban SB749; Giffords 'champion.' As Finance Chair, killed Youngkin's private-school voucher proposal ('not going to happen') and declared his sanctuary-funding-restriction budget item dead. Chief patron of SB438 (expanded Sunday early voting) and SB322 (National Popular Vote compact). No conservative deviations.

claims.text: As Senate President Pro Tempore — the highest-ranking officer in the chamber and the first African American woman to hold that position — Lucas voted YES on SJ247 (January 21, 2025), the constitutional amendment creating an unlimited 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' including abortion care at any stage. All 21 Democratic senators voted with her in the 21-19 party-line passage; Lucas's leadership role made her instrumental in managing the amendment's floor proceedings.

Voted YES on the 2025 Virginia constitutional amendment (first legislative passage) to repeal Article I, Section 15-A of the Virginia Constitution — the provision defining marriage as solely between a man and a woman, enacted by Virginia voters in 2006. The Senate passed the repeal amendment 21-18 on January 21, 2025, with Lucas and her Democratic colleagues providing the margin of passage. The amendment received its required second passage in the 2026 session and went to Virginia voters in November 2026.

Voted YES on SB749 (2026), Virginia's assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, manufacture, and transfer of 'assault firearms' and magazines over 15 rounds. As Senate President Pro Tempore, Lucas presided over the chamber during the 21-19 party-line passage. The bill was strongly opposed by the Virginia Citizens Defense League and Gun Owners of America, who called it an unconstitutional infringement on Second Amendment rights.

Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, 2025 first passage; re-affirmed 2026, headed to Nov 2026 ballot) and is graded A+ / 'Champion' by ReproRising VA; her own site lists 'safeguarding reproductive freedom and access to abortion care' as a priority. Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249, 2025; re-affirmed 2026) and lists 'defending marriage equality and protections for the LGBTQ+ community' as a priority. Voted YES on the felon-voting-rights-restoration amendment. Gun-control record: chief patron of SB173 (2026, firearms banned in hospitals/psychiatric facilities, signed into law) and voted YES on SB1181 (2025 assault-firearms/large-capacity-magazine ban, vetoed by Youngkin). No conservative deviations found. Dimensions with no public record (family/child sovereignty, christian liberty, economic stewardship specifics, federal overreach, border) left NULL — not inferred from party.

profile.religion: Baptist — member of Grove Baptist Church, Portsmouth (Altar Ministry); raised by a pastor father and missionary-teacher mother
profile.background: Democratic VA State Senator, District 21 (City of Norfolk); sworn Jan 2024. Former Norfolk City Council member (2010-2021, Vice-Mayor 2014-2016) and Delegate (HD-90, 2021-2024). Principal broker/owner of a Norfolk real-estate firm. Serves on Senate Privileges & Elections Committee.

candidates.name: Aaron Rouse
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-22 (Virginia Beach), sitting since the Jan 2023 special election. Chairs Senate Privileges & Elections. Lost the June 2025 Democratic LG primary; remains the sitting senator. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Graded A+/'Champion' by ReproRising; voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247) and publicly calls himself 'the deciding vote to protect reproductive freedom.' Voted the Democratic line for the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249). Public-education advocate who opposes school-choice 'diversion' ('focused on ensuring every child can learn and thrive in public schools'); founder of the Rouse's House nonprofit for low-income public-school students. As P&E chair, opposed Youngkin's voter-roll/SAVE-citizenship-verification Executive Order 53 as 'voter suppression … disguised as election integrity' and said fraud is 'virtually nonexistent.' Giffords-endorsed; self-described 'common-sense gun safety' legislator; recorded with the 21-vote Democratic bloc on the 2026 assault-weapons ban (SB749). No conservative deviations (his skill-games bill SB661 was an intra-party gambling matter, vetoed by Gov. Spanberger). Dimensions with no public record (christian liberty, economic-stewardship cells, federal overreach, border, trans/school specifics) left NULL.

Ran for Lieutenant Governor in 2025 and lost the June 2025 Democratic primary; retains his Senate seat.

candidates.name: Mamie Locke
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-23 (Hampton), continuously since 2004; chairs Senate Rules and the Senate Democratic Caucus. Voted the Democratic line for the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249/HJR3, 26-13). claims.text: Voted YES on SJ247 (January 21, 2025), Virginia's constitutional amendment to establish a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' — including abortion care at any stage of pregnancy — with no exceptions for gestational limits, parental consent, or health-neutral regulations. The Senate passed the amendment 21-19 on a strict party-line vote; all 21 Democratic senators, including Locke, voted in favor while all 19 Republicans opposed it. The amendment appeared on the November 2026 statewide ballot after a required second legislative passage.

Voted YES on SB749 (2026 session), Virginia's sweeping assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, importation, manufacture, or transfer of semi-automatic centerfire rifles with detachable magazines and certain features, as well as magazines holding more than 15 rounds. The Senate passed the bill 21-19 on a strict party-line vote, with all 21 Democratic senators — including Locke — voting in favor. Gun Owners of America and the Virginia Citizens Defense League (VCDL) both opposed the bill as unconstitutional. Governor Spanberger signed SB749 into law in May 2026.

candidates.name: Danny Diggs
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-24, sitting since Jan 2024; career sheriff (45+ yrs LE, ~23 yrs York/Poquoson Sheriff). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Solidly conservative record WITH one significant deviation. Pro-life voting: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (both 2025 and 2026), NO on SB1098 (Reproductive & Gender-Affirming Care Act) and on contraception-mandate/abortion-shield bills; ChoiceTracker rates him 'Anti-Choice.' (Disclosure: his STATED personal position favors a 15-week limit with rape/incest/life exceptions, i.e. restrictionist rather than abolitionist — scored against the Durant precedent of consistent pro-life voting = sanctity q0 TRUE.) KEY DEVIATION: voted YES to advance the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment in BOTH the 2025 and 2026 sessions (one of ~5 Senate GOP crossovers) — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. Strongly supports school choice/vouchers/homeschooling and conscience exemptions for religious business owners (iVoterGuide). Career-defining pro-police record (qualified-immunity support; 'put violent criminals behind bars'; chief-patroned child-protection criminal bills SB55/SB399). NRA- and VCDL-endorsed, opposes new gun restrictions. Federal-overreach and several border/election specifics returned no record (NULL).

profile.religion: Baptist / born-again Christian; member of Water's Edge Church
profile.background: Republican VA State Senator, District 24 (York County, Poquoson, Williamsburg, parts of James City & Newport News); won a 2023 upset over the Democratic incumbent, sworn Jan 2024. Spent 45+ years in Peninsula law enforcement, including ~23 years (~5 terms) as elected Sheriff of York County/Poquoson and a term as President of the Virginia Sheriffs' Association.

claims.text: Diggs, a 23-year York County Sheriff and gun owner, ran his 2023 Senate campaign on an explicit Second Amendment platform, pledging that 'the Second Amendment's rights shall not be infringed' and committing to defend those rights against gun-control legislation; as a senator he has voted with the Republican minority against the gun-restriction bills advanced by the Democratic majority in the 2024 and 2025 Virginia General Assembly sessions.

Diggs stated in his 2023 Senate campaign that he supports Governor Youngkin's 15-week abortion limit with exceptions for rape, incest, and the health of the mother — a position that accepts elective abortion through 15 weeks gestation and retains rape/incest exceptions, falling short of the rubric's personhood-from-conception and no-exception standard for this question.

candidates.name: Richard Stuart
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-25 (Northern Neck/Stafford), in the Senate since 2007. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Conservative record that HELD THE LINE on the social amendments. Voted NAY on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment) — confirmed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call — and NAY on the House companion (ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice'; National Right to Life endorsement 2019). UNLIKE Diggs/Durant, voted NAY on SJ249 (same-sex-marriage amendment) — confirmed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call — and was NOT among the 2026 GOP crossovers, so NO marriage deviation. On immigration he backed enforcement measures in 2025 and supports State Police-ICE (287g-style) cooperation, dismissing benefits for those 'here unlawfully.' Pro-2A: vocally opposed the 2020 'red flag' law (SB240) on due-process grounds; campaign 'strong supporter of our 2nd Amendment.' Family/child-sovereignty, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach specifics, and interest-group letter grades returned no citable record and are NULL — not inferred from party. (Sparse-but-uniformly-aligned record: 5 TRUE / 0 FALSE.)

Known statewide for environmental / Chesapeake Bay work; former chair of the Senate Agriculture, Conservation & Natural Resources Committee.

claims.text: As Virginia State Senate Minority Leader, has publicly and repeatedly committed to always defending 'the lives of the unborn.' Voted against Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment (2026), which passed the Senate 21-19 on a strict party-line vote with McDougle casting one of the 19 Republican 'no' votes. The amendment heads to a November 2026 referendum and, if passed by voters, would enshrine a near-unlimited abortion right in the state constitution.

Voted against Virginia state budget proposals that would have increased direct appropriations to Planned Parenthood — the nation's largest abortion provider — defunding a key pillar of the abortion industry's operations at the state level.

As Senate Minority Leader, led unified Republican opposition to the 2026 sweeping gun-control package, including the assault-weapons ban (SB 749) that passed the Virginia Senate 21-19. Criticized the legislation's unintended consequences for lawful gun owners and helped coordinate the 19-vote Republican bloc — representing every Republican senator — that held firm against the most expansive gun-restriction package in Virginia history, signed by Gov. Spanberger on May 15, 2026.

profile.background: Virginia State Senator, District 27 (Fredericksburg/Stafford/Spotsylvania), sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate HD-28 (2022-2024). Educator/librarian; 'Marine wife,' three children. Now running for U.S. House VA-7 (R primary Aug 2026).

claims.text: One of 19 VA Senate Republicans who voted against the all-trimester abortion constitutional amendment (SJ1, 2025 and 2026 sessions), unanimously opposing the Democratic majority's effort to enshrine unrestricted abortion through the third trimester into Virginia's constitution.

Introduced a floor amendment to the Virginia abortion constitutional amendment that would have added parental rights protections — requiring parental involvement in minors' abortion decisions — which Democrats voted down, demonstrating Durant's commitment to parental authority over children's medical decisions.

As a Republican in the Virginia House of Delegates (2022–2024) and then state Senate (2024–present), Durant consistently voted against gun-control packages advanced by Virginia Democrats, including legislation to create red-flag (extreme risk protection order) laws and expand background-check mandates. The Virginia Citizens Defense League (VCDL) — which grades every member of the General Assembly on gun votes — lists Durant as a Second Amendment defender for opposing new firearm restrictions; she opposes red-flag ERPOs, assault-weapons bans, and magazine-capacity limits. Her 2026 congressional campaign platform identifies protecting Second Amendment rights as a core commitment.

On January 16, 2026, Durant voted YES on HJR3 in the Virginia Senate — a joint resolution placing a constitutional amendment on the November 2026 ballot that would repeal Virginia's existing constitutional provision defining marriage as solely the union of one man and one woman, replacing it with language recognizing marriages 'without regard to sex, gender, or race.' The Senate passed the measure 26-13; Durant was one of exactly four Republicans who crossed party lines to vote yes alongside Democrats, while the rest of the Republican caucus voted no. No public statement by Durant explaining her vote appeared in any indexed news source. Her yes vote is the operative record: she voted to remove Virginia's constitutional recognition of one-man-one-woman marriage from state law — the opposite of the rubric's standard.

Durant's 2025-2026 congressional campaign (she suspended the bid May 22, 2026) explicitly committed to a Balanced Budget Amendment as a named federal legislative priority. Her campaign website (taradurant.com/meet-tara/) stated she would 'fight for pro-growth, America-first policies — including a Balanced Budget Amendment, permanent Trump tax cuts for American families, and ending taxes on tips, overtime, and Social Security.' Her June 18, 2025 launch statement, carried by Potomac Local, said: 'Now I'm running for Congress to take that same fight to Washington and work with President Trump to secure our border, fully fund our police, end the reckless spending, and restore common-sense leadership.' Her X announcement described partnering with Gov. Youngkin 'to cut taxes, back the blue, and grow our economy.' A named Balanced Budget Amendment commitment is the clearest possible alignment with the rubric's anti-deficit standard.

Durant's congressional campaign website (taradurant.com/issues/) stated she would 'fight to keep poison out of our communities, build the wall, and end taxpayer-funded benefits for illegal immigrants,' and that 'Tara knows open borders bring crime, trafficking, and deadly drugs like fentanyl.' Her June 2025 campaign launch included a pledge to 'work with President Trump to secure our border, balance the budget, and put America first,' covered in the Fredericksburg Free Press and Potomac Local. The explicit border-wall pledge combined with ending taxpayer subsidies for illegal immigrants and invoking Trump's full border agenda places her squarely within the rubric's wall-and-enforcement standard.

McPike has served Senate District 29 (Prince William, Fauquier, Stafford counties) since January 2016.

Voted YES on the Senate passage of HJR 3 (Remove Constitutional Same-Sex Marriage Ban Amendment) on January 16, 2026 — a 26-13 vote in which all 21 Senate Democrats (including McPike) plus 5 Republicans voted YES, 13 Republicans NO — placing a constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex marriage ban on the November 2026 ballot.

Virginia Senate Democrats voted unanimously for the 2026 gun-control package. McPike voted YES on HB 217 when the Senate passed it on March 9, 2026 (21-Y 19-N, all 21 Senate Democrats YES, 19 Republicans NO) — the bill banning purchase, sale, transfer, and possession of commonly owned semi-automatic firearms, limiting magazines, and restricting legal adults under 21. The bill was sent to Governor Spanberger following House concurrence on March 11, 2026.

claims.text: Russet Perry campaigned and serves on a platform of protecting abortion access, stating: 'Like most voters in our commonwealth, I support the current law as it stands,' and 'I will be the vote to protect the right to choose.' She positioned herself as the decisive vote against any abortion restrictions, framing her opponent's vote for a 15-week ban as the pro-restriction threat she would block — directly opposing the life-at-conception and personhood standard the rubric supports.

Perry championed and passed legislation restricting firearm purchases for individuals convicted of assault and battery of a family or household member or intimate partner — expanding the class of disqualifying offenses beyond federal law. She votes consistently with the Virginia Senate Democratic caucus on gun-restriction measures, opposing the rubric's anti-restriction and constitutional-carry principles.

claims.text: Publicly and emphatically committed to preserving unrestricted abortion access in Virginia during his January 2025 special-election campaign: 'Virginia is the last, the only state in the South where women don't have to worry about [the legality of abortion]. And I am very, very committed to keeping it that way.' This stance explicitly rejects any statutory protection for unborn life, contrary to the rubric's life-at-conception standard.

Voted YES on SJ247 on January 21, 2025 — just six days after being sworn in to the Senate on January 15, 2025, following his victory in the SD-32 special election. SJ247 constitutionally enshrines an unlimited 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' that bars the Commonwealth from restricting abortion at any stage, with the Senate passing the amendment 21-19 on a strict party-line vote. Srinivasan's vote was his first major Senate action after taking office.

Voted YES on SB749 (2026 session), Virginia's assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, importation, manufacture, or transfer of semi-automatic centerfire rifles with detachable magazines and certain features, and magazines holding more than 15 rounds. The Senate passed the bill 21-19 on a party-line vote, with Srinivasan joining all 20 other Democratic senators in favor. Gun Owners of America condemned the bill as 'unconstitutional' and urged Virginians to contact their representatives in opposition.

claims.text: As Virginia Senate Majority Leader, Scott Surovell declared upon taking leadership: 'We need to get Roe v. Wade into the constitution of Virginia.' In January 2025 he supported the constitutional amendment recognizing Virginians' fundamental right to reproductive freedom — encompassing abortion, contraception, miscarriage management, and fertility care — directly rejecting any life-at-conception or personhood standard.

In January 2025, Surovell co-sponsored the constitutional amendment to repeal Virginia's 2006 marriage amendment that defined marriage as 'only a union between a man and a woman,' clearing the way to remove the one-man-one-woman definition from the Virginia Constitution and replace it with no-restriction language. He is a vocal champion of same-sex marriage rights, directly rejecting the biblical definition of marriage the rubric upholds.

claims.text: Voted yes on SJ1 (Virginia Senate abortion-rights constitutional amendment, 2026 Regular Session), which passed 21-18 on a straight party-line vote, placing a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' amendment on Virginia's November 2026 ballot. The amendment allows unrestricted abortion through the second trimester and broad third-trimester exceptions, rejecting personhood from conception.

Voted yes on the Senate version of the same-sex marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249/HJ9, 2026 session) placing a measure on the November 2026 ballot to enshrine same-sex and interracial marriage in Virginia's constitution, overturning the existing one-man-one-woman constitutional definition.

Consistently backs Virginia Senate gun-control legislation. In the 2026 session, the Senate panel advanced gun-safety bills previously vetoed by Gov. Youngkin — including expanded red-flag law enforcement, safe-storage mandates, and background-check enhancements — on a path Marsden supported as a Democratic member.

candidates.name: Stella Pekarsky
candidates.notes: VA State Senator, SD-36 (Fairfax); sitting since Jan 2024; former Fairfax County School Board chair. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Reliably progressive record. SANCTITY OF LIFE: patron of and voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247 SV89); ReproRising 'A+'; ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice'; sponsored SB137 (2026, clinic-protection); on record '100% pro-choice'; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA + EMILY's List. MARRIAGE/GENDER: in the Democratic bloc on the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249); voted YES on SB1098 (Reproductive & Gender-Affirming Health Care Act); OPPOSED 'Sage's Law' (parental notification on gender transition) and moved to reject Youngkin's gender-privacy budget amendment. FAMILY/CHILD: her opposition to parental-notification-on-gender (Sage's Law) is a documented contrary position on parental sovereignty. ECONOMICS: party-line support for the $15 minimum wage (SB1). IMMIGRATION: co-patron of SB491 (bars schools disclosing student data to immigration) and SB351 (limits ICE in 'protected areas'). SELF-DEFENSE: Giffords-endorsed; sponsored SB115 (2026, restrict out-of-state CCW reciprocity) and SB255 (2024, safe-storage notification). Christian-liberty (SB1031 ambiguous), federal-overreach, election (voter-ID/mail-in), and police/bail public-justice cells = NO clean public record, left NULL.

profile.religion: Greek Orthodox Christian (per Wikipedia; married to Pavel Pekarsky; Greek descent)
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-36 (Fairfax County), since Jan 2024 (defeated incumbent George Barker in the 2023 primary). Former FCPS teacher; Fairfax County School Board member (Sully District) 2020-2023 and Board Chair 2021-22. B.A. and M.Ed., George Mason University. Ran for U.S. House (VA-11) in the 2025 primary and lost; retains her state Senate seat.

claims.text: Stella Pekarsky sponsored Virginia's constitutional amendment to recognize Virginians' fundamental right to reproductive freedom — encompassing pre- and post-natal care, contraception, abortion, miscarriage management, and fertility care. She also sponsored legislation prohibiting the extradition of Virginians to other states for abortion services performed legally in Virginia, and a bill prohibiting collection of menstrual health data. Virginia's Choice Tracker rates her explicitly as Pro-Choice.

Reducing gun violence is a stated legislative priority for Pekarsky; as part of the January 2025 Fairfax delegation's agenda she co-introduced gun-control legislation alongside abortion and election-reform bills. She votes consistently with the Virginia Senate Democratic caucus on gun restrictions and opposes the constitutional-carry and anti-restriction principles the rubric supports.

claims.text: Saddam Azlan Salim voted to pass — and co-sponsored — Virginia's constitutional amendment recognizing Virginians' fundamental right to reproductive freedom, encompassing abortion, contraception, miscarriage management, and fertility care. Virginia's Choice Tracker rates him explicitly as Pro-Choice. He stated the amendment would enshrine reproductive rights in the state constitution, 'safeguarding these freedoms from potential future assaults by the General Assembly,' directly rejecting any life-at-conception or personhood standard.

Senator Salim introduced SB848 to raise the age to purchase an assault firearm to 21 years old, and SB891 to impose a five-day waiting period before any firearm purchase — explicitly arguing both measures would reduce gun homicides and suicides. These bills directly oppose the rubric's anti-restriction and anti-waiting-period principles protecting Second Amendment rights.

claims.text: As part of the unanimous Virginia Senate Democratic caucus, voted YES on HJR 1 (Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment) on its Senate passage — a 26-13 vote in which all 21 Senate Democrats (including Boysko) plus 5 Republicans voted YES, 13 Republicans NO — placing a constitutional abortion-rights amendment on the November 2026 ballot. Boysko represents Senate District 38 in Loudoun County.

Voted YES on the Senate passage of HJR 3 (Remove Constitutional Same-Sex Marriage Ban Amendment) on January 16, 2026 — a 26-13 vote in which all 21 Senate Democrats (including Boysko) plus 5 Republicans voted YES, 13 Republicans NO — placing a constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex marriage ban on the November 2026 ballot.

Virginia Senate Democrats voted unanimously for the 2026 gun-control package. Boysko voted YES on HB 217 when the Senate passed it on March 9, 2026 (21-Y 19-N, all 21 Senate Democrats YES, 19 Republicans NO) — banning purchase, sale, transfer, and possession of commonly owned semi-automatic firearms, limiting magazines, and restricting legal adults under 21. The bill was sent to Governor Spanberger after House concurrence on March 11, 2026.

claims.text: Patrick Hope chaired the Virginia House Public Safety Committee and was the lead architect of Virginia's landmark 2020 gun control package, which enacted universal background checks, mandatory reporting of lost or stolen firearms, a one-handgun-per-month purchase limit, and red-flag/extreme risk protective orders allowing courts to temporarily confiscate firearms from individuals deemed a risk. He endorsed and voted for the 2026 package banning assault-style weapon sales — a consistent record directly opposing the rubric's opposition to red-flag laws and AWB/magazine restrictions.

Patrick Hope voted for Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment (HJR 1) in both the 2025 and 2026 General Assembly sessions, and publicly stated he supports 'the right to a safe and legal abortion' and opposes any 'attempt to legislate a patient's decision to end a pregnancy.' He has also served as patron of abortion-access legislation in the General Assembly and is listed as a legislative champion by REPRO Rising Virginia — directly rejecting a personhood-from-conception standard.

candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-2 (Arlington/NoVA), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: sponsored/voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJR1/SJR247) and contraception-access bills; voted NO on restricting public abortion funding (HB404); EMILY's List 2025 award + REPRO Rising endorsement. Co-patron + YES on HJ9 (repeal same-sex-marriage ban); campaign pledges to expand statewide LGBTQ+ protections. Public-education-funding advocate (opposes diverting funds to school choice). Reform-oriented on justice ('supportive, not punitive' responses). Strongly pro-gun-control: sponsored mandatory firearm-locking-device bill (HB2064/HB158) and the dating-partner-loophole bill (HB1869); Giffords/Brady/Moms Demand endorsed. No conservative deviations found. Score 0/100 — all scored cells documented contrary to the rubric; thinner dimensions (Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration, much of elections) left null for lack of a named vote.

claims.text: McClure sponsored legislation in the Virginia General Assembly to prohibit the purchase, possession, or transportation of firearms by persons convicted of assault and battery of a family or household member or intimate partner — a new class of misdemeanor-based firearm restriction. The bill was vetoed by Governor Youngkin. Her effort to expand the categories of persons stripped of gun rights and her stated aim of 'preventing gun violence' directly conflict with the rubric's opposition to new firearm purchase restrictions.

McClure voted for HJR 1 — the Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment — when it passed the Virginia House 51-48 on 14 January 2025 on a strict party-line vote, with all 51 Democrats in favor. Her support for this measure, combined with her broader progressive legislative agenda, confirms her rejection of any life-from-conception standard in Virginia law.

candidates.name: Alfonso Lopez
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-3 (Arlington/Alexandria), sitting since 2012; founder of the VA Latino Caucus; chairs Agriculture/Natural Resources. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deep, uniformly progressive record across nearly every dimension. Sanctity: NARAL 100% lifetime, co-patron 2020 Reproductive Health Protection Act. Marriage: recorded YEA on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026) same-sex-marriage amendment; chief patron of VA Human Rights Act expansion + an LGBTQ advisory board. Schools: chief patron HB913 reversing the transgender-student model policies; opposes charter schools. Justice: bans on no-knock warrants/chokeholds, limited mandatory minimums, helped abolish the death penalty. Immigration (his signature): DREAM Act, driver-privilege cards (HB911), anti-ICE bills HB1440/HB1441; clearly pro-immigrant/sanctuary-aligned. Guns: chief patron of concealed-permit-competence (HB916) + firearm tax bills; backs universal background checks. NO conservative deviation. Score 0/100. His 'resist federal authority' work is ANTI-ICE (opposite valence) so federal-overreach cells are NOT credited — left null. Christian-liberty/CBDC/ESG/E-Verify/birthright/IVF have no public record — null.

claims.text: Lopez spent years championing in-state college tuition for undocumented immigrants in Virginia, modeling his bills after the federal DREAM Act. In 2020 that legislation finally passed into law (HB 1547), granting taxpayer-subsidized tuition access to individuals residing in the country illegally. Lopez celebrated this as a marquee achievement. This posture — granting public benefits to unlawful residents — directly conflicts with the rubric's mandatory-deportation and enforcement standards.

As House Democratic Whip (2016-2022) and a long-serving progressive from Northern Virginia's Arlington/Alexandria suburbs, Lopez voted for HJR 1 — the Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment — when it passed the Virginia House 51-48 on a strict party-line vote on 14 January 2025. The amendment would entrench a sweeping constitutional right to abortion at all stages of pregnancy, rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard.

claims.text: Primary sponsor of Virginia's HJ1 abortion-rights constitutional amendment (introduced in the 2025 and 2026 Regular Sessions). Herring's amendment enshrines 'the fundamental right to reproductive freedom' in the state constitution, permits abortion through the second trimester without restriction and through the third with broad health-based exceptions, and explicitly rejects any personhood-from-conception standard. The House passed the final version 64-34 in January 2026.

Voted yes on HJ9 (2026 session) placing a constitutional amendment to enshrine same-sex and interracial marriage on Virginia's November 2026 ballot, repealing the 2006 constitutional provision defining marriage as between one man and one woman.

As House Majority Leader, helped drive the February 2026 gun-control package through the House over unanimous Republican opposition, a bill package including provisions expanding background checks, restricting assault-style weapons, imposing magazine-capacity limits, and strengthening red-flag laws.

claims.text: As part of the unanimous Virginia House Democratic caucus, voted YES on HJR 1 (Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment) on January 14, 2025 — a 51-48 party-line vote in which all 51 Democrats supported constitutionalizing abortion as a fundamental right — and again voted YES on second passage in January 2026 (64-34, all 64 Democrats YES), placing the amendment on the November 2026 ballot. McPike represents House District 5 in Prince William County.

Voted YES on HJR 3 (January 14, 2026, second passage, 67-31 with all 64 House Democrats plus 3 Republicans voting YES, 31 Republicans NO) to repeal Virginia's constitutional same-sex marriage ban and replace it with a mandate recognizing marriages without regard to sex or gender, advancing the amendment to the November 2026 ballot.

Virginia Citizens Defense League records confirm Virginia House Democrats voted unanimously for all gun-control legislation in recent sessions. McPike voted YES on HB 217 (February 5, 2026, 58-34) — the 2026 'assault firearms' ban prohibiting purchase, sale, transfer, and possession of commonly owned semi-automatic rifles, pistols, and shotguns while limiting magazine capacity — as part of the Democrat-led anti-gun package headed to Governor Spanberger's desk.

claims.text: Rip Sullivan voted for Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment (HJR 1) in both the 2025 and 2026 General Assembly sessions. The amendment — codifying abortion, contraception, fertility care, and miscarriage management as constitutional rights — passed the House 64-34 along party lines, with Sullivan voting yes as a member of the Democratic caucus, directly rejecting any life-at-conception or personhood protection.

Rip Sullivan voted for the Virginia constitutional amendment (HJR 9) to remove the state's 2006 provision defining marriage as 'only a union between a man and a woman,' advancing the measure to the 2026 ballot. Sullivan is also the original author of the Virginia Clean Economy Act, which mandates 100% carbon-free electricity by 2050 — requiring businesses to align energy use with government-dictated ESG-style targets — consistent with the progressive caucus agenda he votes with on social definitions of marriage.

candidates.name: Karen Keys-Gamarra
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-7 (Fairfax County); attorney, former Fairfax County School Board member; reelected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent progressive record on the flagship 2026-ballot amendments: co-patron/supporter of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, House 51-48 in 2025; advanced again 64-34 in 2026) and the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (HJ3, House 67-31). Co-sponsor of the felon voting-rights restoration amendment (HJ2). Strongly pro-gun-control: CHIEF PATRON of HB207 (2026), a $500 retail tax on firearm suppressors, and a patron of Del. Helmer's HB217 assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban; endorsed by Everytown/Moms Demand Action. CROSS-CURRENT NOTE (documented, but maps to no rubric cell): chief patron of HB994 (2024), the bill banning child marriage by setting the marriage age at 18 with no exceptions — bipartisan, signed by Republican Gov. Youngkin; a genuine child-protection law. No conservative deviations on the scored dimensions. Cells with no public record (Christian liberty, federal overreach, immigration roll calls, election-admin specifics) left null, not inferred.

claims.text: As part of the unanimous Virginia House Democratic caucus, voted YES on HJR 1 (Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment) on January 14, 2025 (51-48 party-line) and again YES in January 2026 (64-34) to place the abortion-rights constitutional amendment on the November 2026 ballot. Keys-Gamarra previously served on the Fairfax County School Board (2017-2024) before her election to the House in 2023.

VCDL 2025 voting records confirm Virginia House Democrats voted unanimously against all pro-gun bills and for all gun-control legislation in the 2025 session, including a package banning commonly owned semi-automatic firearms. Keys-Gamarra (HD-7, Fairfax) voted with her caucus in opposition to Second Amendment rights.

candidates.name: Irene Shin
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-8 (Fairfax County); former nonprofit director; reelected Nov 2025; lost the 2025 VA-11 congressional primary, remains the sitting delegate. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive record: voted YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, party-line 51-48), the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9, 58-35), and the felon voting-rights amendment (HJ2, 55-44). Rated a 'Pro-Choice Champion' by Virginia Choice Tracker; member of the Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia and NOW-VA. Co-patron of the 2024 assault-weapons ban (HB2); voted for a firearm-violence-prevention office (HB1736); Giffords PAC 'gun safety champion.' Cosponsored the $15 minimum-wage bill (HB1, 2024, vetoed). No conservative deviations found. Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, and immigration roll calls had no citable Shin-specific vote and are left null, not inferred.

claims.text: Voted yes on HJ1 (64-34 party-line House vote, January 2026 Regular Session) placing a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' constitutional amendment on Virginia's November 2026 ballot, allowing unrestricted abortion through the second trimester and broad third-trimester exceptions while rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard.

Voted for Virginia House Democrats' February 2026 gun-control package — passed over unanimous Republican opposition — which included assault-weapon restrictions, red-flag law enhancements, magazine-capacity limits, and expanded universal background-check requirements.

Voted yes on HJ9 (2026 session) for the same-sex and interracial marriage constitutional amendment to appear on Virginia's November 2026 ballot, repealing the state constitution's existing one-man-one-woman marriage definition.

candidates.name: Karrie Delaney
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-9 (western Fairfax); in office since 2017; reelected Nov 2025; 98% Democratic-caucus unity (VPAP). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Deeply documented progressive record from her individual LIS roll calls: YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1, 2025 & 2026), the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026), the $15 minimum-wage bill (HB1), and a full slate of ballot-access expansions (Sunday early voting SB438, extended absentee-receipt deadline HB82, ballot-cure HB773/774, automatic-voter-registration work group HB319, ranked-choice HB630). Voted YES on HB1441 limiting local law-enforcement (287g) cooperation with federal immigration authorities. Gun-control: YES on the HB217 assault-weapons/large-magazine ban, red-flag reporting (HB1096), safe-storage (HB871/HB201), and ghost-gun ban (HB40); Giffords PAC-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT NOTE (routine near-unanimous votes, left NULL per the high-bar credit rule, not counted as alignment): YES on SB778 (display of obscene material to a minor unlawful, 96-0), HB131 (religious-observance accommodation at public colleges, 94-2), and several minor pro-law-enforcement administrative bills. No genuine conservative deviation on any scored dimension.

candidates.name: Dan Helmer
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-10 (Fairfax County); Army veteran (West Point, LTC, ret.); House Dem Caucus Campaigns Chair; running for Congress (VA-7, 2026). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] A leading progressive: CHIEF PATRON of HB217 (2026), the assault-weapons + >15-round-magazine ban now signed into law (Chapter 1106) — his quote: 'weapons similar to those I carried in Iraq and Afghanistan should not be trafficked in our commonwealth.' Multiply Giffords/Brady-endorsed. Co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1) and the felon voting-rights amendment (HJ2), both headed to the Nov 2026 ballot; Planned Parenthood-endorsed. CORRECTION captured during research: an iVoterGuide-derived snippet suggesting he 'supports school choice' is a misread — he did NOT answer that questionnaire; not scored. DISCIPLINE NOTE: his individual same-sex-marriage roll call could not be confirmed (he is not a patron of the marriage amendment and LIS vote pages are JS-rendered), so biblical_marriage is left null rather than inferred from party. Christian-liberty, federal-overreach, family/child, and immigration cells had no confirmed Helmer-specific vote and are null.

claims.text: Chief patron of Virginia HB 217 (2026 Regular Session) — the most sweeping assault-firearms ban in Virginia history, signed and enacted May 14, 2026 — which makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor to import, sell, manufacture, purchase, or transfer assault firearms, and bans magazines holding more than 15 rounds. Any person convicted is barred from purchasing, possessing, or transporting a firearm for three years. Helmer shepherded the bill through both chambers of the Democrat-controlled General Assembly; it takes effect July 1, 2026.

In his 2024 Democratic primary campaign for Virginia's 10th Congressional District, Helmer named 'expanding access to abortion' as a core policy priority alongside gun control. He has also supported the Virginia Legislature's 2025-2026 effort to enshrine a state constitutional right to reproductive freedom including abortion; the proposed amendment cleared both chambers of the General Assembly and will appear on the 2026 Virginia ballot.

candidates.name: Gretchen Bulova
candidates.notes: VA Delegate HD-11 (City of Fairfax + part of Fairfax County); sworn in Jan 14, 2026 via special election to succeed her husband David Bulova; career museum administrator, no prior elected office. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, REPRO Rising VA, and Moms Demand Action. On all four contested culture-war roll calls she voted with the Democratic majority; no cross-party deviation. claims.text: Vivian Watts has served in the Virginia House of Delegates since 1988 and consistently supports abortion access. During the 2024 session she co-sponsored HB 78 — companion to SB 16 — prohibiting law enforcement from subpoenaing menstrual health data stored on third-party apps (a measure aimed at protecting abortion-seekers from prosecution). She voted to prohibit the Board of Medicine from disciplining physicians for performing legal abortion services, rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard. REPRO Rising Virginia, the statewide reproductive-rights lobby, lists her as a key legislative ally.

During the 2024 Virginia session, Watts voted to prohibit the public carrying of assault-style weapons in the Commonwealth — a direct restriction on the type of commonly owned semi-automatic firearms the rubric identifies as protected under the Second Amendment. She is identified by the Progressive Voters Guide as a champion of gun violence prevention, consistently supporting legislation restricting firearms in public spaces and expanding background checks.

claims.text: Rozia Henson voted for Virginia's Right to Reproductive Freedom constitutional amendment (HJR 1) in both the 2025 and 2026 General Assembly sessions. The amendment — which enshrines abortion, contraception, miscarriage management, and fertility care as constitutionally protected rights — passed the House 64-34 along party lines, with Democrats (including Henson) voting yes. It is now on the November 2026 ballot, directly rejecting any life-at-conception or personhood standard.

Rozia Henson is the first openly gay Black man elected to the Virginia General Assembly. He voted for Virginia's constitutional amendment (HJR 9) to repeal the state's 2006 provision defining marriage as 'only a union between a man and a woman' — a measure advanced to the 2026 ballot with near-unanimous Democratic support. This directly opposes the one-man-one-woman definition of marriage the rubric upholds.

She had previously described gun violence reduction as 'a top priority' and cited her experience as a Prince William County supervisor working with law enforcement to reduce gun violence.

claims.text: Voted YES on HJ1 (January 14, 2025), Virginia's constitutional resolution to establish a 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' including abortion care at any stage of pregnancy, with no gestational limit. The House passed HJ1 51-48 on a near-party-line vote, with Martinez joining the Democratic majority in favor while all Republicans opposed. The resolution initiated the required two-session constitutional amendment process; the second passage was completed in January 2026.

Voted YES on the Virginia constitutional resolution (January 14, 2025) to repeal Article I, Section 15-A — the 2006 voter-approved provision defining marriage in Virginia as solely between one man and one woman. The House passed the repeal 58-35, with Martinez and 50 other Democrats voting in favor alongside seven Republicans. The amendment received its required second passage in the 2026 session and was placed on the November 2026 ballot for voter ratification.

Voted YES on SB749 (March 4, 2026), Virginia's assault-firearms ban prohibiting the purchase, sale, importation, manufacture, or transfer of semi-automatic centerfire rifles with detachable magazines and certain features, and magazines holding more than 15 rounds. The House passed the bill 59-35 on a near-party-line vote; Martinez voted with the Democratic majority in favor. The bill was condemned by Gun Owners of America and the Virginia Citizens Defense League as unconstitutional. Governor Spanberger signed it into law in May 2026.

claims.text: As part of the unanimous Virginia House Democratic caucus, voted YES on HJR 1 (Virginia Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment) on January 14, 2025 (51-48, all 51 Democrats YES) and again on second passage in January 2026 (64-34, all 64 Democrats YES), placing a constitutional abortion-rights amendment on the November 2026 ballot. McAuliff represents House District 30 in Fairfax County.

Voted YES on HJR 3 (January 14, 2026, second passage, 67-31 with all 64 House Democrats voting YES) to repeal Virginia's constitutional same-sex marriage ban and require the state to recognize marriages without regard to sex or gender, placing the amendment on the November 2026 ballot.

VCDL voting records confirm Virginia House Democrats voted unanimously against every pro-gun bill and for every gun-control bill in the 2025 and 2026 sessions. McAuliff voted YES on HB 217 (February 5, 2026, 58-34) — banning commonly owned semi-automatic firearms and limiting magazine capacities — as part of the Democrat-led 2026 anti-gun package that headed to Governor Spanberger's desk after the legislature adjourned March 14, 2026.
