claims.text: Established a 16-year staunchly anti-abortion record in the Virginia House of Delegates before Congress; in the House voted consistently to stop taxpayer dollars from funding abortion domestically and internationally, cosponsored the Pain-Capable Unborn Children from Late-Term Abortions Act, and pushed back on Biden-era pro-abortion executive actions — matching the rubric's life-at-conception and protection-of-the-unborn standard.

Voted for H.R.2, the Secure the Border Act of 2023, which funds border-wall construction, tightens asylum standards, and expands enforcement capacity; has repeatedly called Biden's open-border policies a national crisis and stated that 'every town' in Virginia has become a border town due to fentanyl, human trafficking, and illegal immigration.

Cosponsored legislation demanding the FBI and DOJ purge firearms-related records on individuals who were coerced or unduly pressured into waiving their gun rights — a pro-Second Amendment position consistent with the rubric's rejection of background-check overreach and firearm registries.

As Chair of the Republican Study Committee Budget & Spending Taskforce, Cline authored the 'Fiscal Sanity to Save America' proposal that would balance the federal budget within seven years by cutting $16.7 trillion over ten years. He has consistently voted against large deficit-financed spending packages — including a 'no' vote on the $4 trillion debt-ceiling hike — and earned the Club for Growth 'Defender of Economic Freedom' award for maintaining a 90%+ anti-spending score across 2022, 2023, and 2024.

Voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (H.R. 1, May 2025) which includes mandatory deportation enforcement: enhanced detention capacity, expanded removal authority, and mandatory ICE cooperation for criminal aliens — consistent with the rubric's standard of mandatory deportation. Cline stated the bill 'secures our border' as one of the core promises House Republicans made.

Cline cosponsored H.R. 1919, the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (119th Congress), which prohibits the Federal Reserve from issuing a retail central bank digital currency or maintaining individual financial accounts. The bill passed the House 219–210 on July 17, 2025, with Cline's co-sponsorship on record — blocking a government-issued digital dollar that would enable unprecedented real-time financial surveillance of American citizens.

Cline voted for H.R. 3492, the Protect Children's Innocence Act of 2025 (House Vote #351, December 17, 2025, 216–211), which establishes federal criminal penalties for physicians who perform gender-transition surgeries or administer cross-sex hormones or puberty-blocking drugs to minors — rejecting gender ideology and affirming biological sex as immutable in federal law.

candidates.name: Eugene Vindman
candidates.notes:
U.S. Rep, VA-07 (D), since Jan 2025. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Solidly progressive on most issues with several documented conservative-aligned deviations. HARD: Heritage Action 0% lifetime; cosponsor of WHPA (H.R.12, 119th); Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2026 endorsement; Giffords + Everytown endorsed (supports universal background checks, national red-flag, AWB-era restrictions); NO on Laken Riley Act (first vote as Member); NO on SAVE Act (H.R.22); joined call to impeach DHS Sec Noem; opposed Stafford ICE detention center; Ukraine/Russia hawk. DEVIATION (parental rights / family sovereignty): voted YES on H.R.2616 'Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids Act' (parental consent before schools change a student's pronouns / sex-based accommodations) — one of only 8 Democrats; his press release: 'Vindman Votes to Keep Parents Involved.' DEVIATION (foreign-policy restraint): voted YES on Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll Call 85, 119th) explicitly citing Article I Section 8. DEVIATION (industry capture / small-farm): cosponsor of LOCAL Foods Act with Rep. Baird (R-IN) (direct-to-consumer livestock sales, USDA inspection exemption); voted NO on Farm Bill citing pesticide-manufacturer liability shield (Bayer/Monsanto). Sitting member; running for reelection 2026 (Aug 4 primary). Note: he briefly announced (Feb 2026) intent to run in a redrawn VA-01 under a mid-decade map, but the VA Supreme Court struck down the referendum (May 2026), so the race reverts to VA-07.

Prior internal confidence_note that said simple YEA on Laken Riley was incomplete/wrong for the first vote. claims.text: Campaigned on a pledge to 'protect reproductive freedom' by stopping any national abortion ban and passing legislation to restore abortion access, rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard.

Voted in February 2026 for a sweeping House Democratic gun-control package, including the Law-Enforcement Innovate to De-Escalate Act providing nonlethal-option funding, and publicly backed 'commonsense policies … getting illegal guns off streets' — opposing the rubric's defense of unrestricted Second Amendment rights.

Came to the U.S. as a Soviet Jewish refugee and consistently favors expanded legal-immigration pathways and humanitarian protections; opposes mandatory mass deportation, aligning with open-border rather than enforcement-first immigration policy — contra the rubric's mandatory-deportation standard.

On March 5, 2026, Vindman voted in favor of H.Con.Res. 38 (House Vote #85), a War Powers Resolution directing the President to remove U.S. Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities against Iran unless Congress explicitly authorizes them — consistent with the rubric's Article I war-powers standard. Vindman stated the Iran conflict has 'no legal justification under domestic and international law.'

Vindman voted NO on H.R. 22, the SAVE Act (House Vote #102, April 10, 2025), which required documentary proof of U.S. citizenship as a condition of federal voter registration. The bill passed 220–208 on a near-party-line vote; Vindman's opposition is consistent with the Democratic position opposing proof-of-citizenship requirements — at odds with the rubric's voter-ID and election-integrity standard.

claims.text: Cosponsored H.R. 3755, the Women's Health Protection Act of 2021, to codify a federal abortion right through viability into federal law — rejecting any personhood-from-conception standard.

In June 2022, introduced a bill to impose a 1,000% excise tax on assault-style rifles and high-capacity magazines as 'another creative pathway to actually make some sensible gun control happen,' and cosponsored H.R. 1808, the Assault Weapons Ban of 2022 — opposing the rubric's defense of unrestricted Second Amendment rights.

Voted YES on H.R. 8404, the Respect for Marriage Act (2022), codifying federal recognition of same-sex marriage and overriding any future Supreme Court reversal of Obergefell — rejecting the one-man-one-woman definition of marriage.

Beyer voted NO on H.R. 22, the SAVE Act (House Vote #102, April 10, 2025), which required documentary proof of U.S. citizenship for federal voter registration. The bill passed 220–208 on a near-party-line vote. Beyer's voting record is among the most consistently partisan in the Virginia delegation (missed fewer than 2% of votes, voting with Democrats nearly 100% of the time per GovTrack), making his opposition to the rubric's voter-ID standard clear.

Beyer voted YES on H.R. 1319 (American Rescue Plan Act, March 10, 2021) and H.R. 5376 (Inflation Reduction Act, August 12, 2022) — two major deficit-funded spending packages totaling over $2.5 trillion. As a member of the Joint Economic Committee, Beyer has championed federal investment as economic strategy and has not supported balanced budget amendments, placing him in direct opposition to the rubric's anti-deficit/balanced-budget standard.

claims.text: Holds a 100% rating from the National Right to Life Committee and 0% from Planned Parenthood Action Fund. After Dobbs (2022), called Roe v. Wade 'a constitutional error that has produced decades of tragedy' and welcomed the ruling returning abortion law to the states.

Introduced H.R. 4460, the NO VOTE for Non-Citizens Act of 2023 (118th Congress), prohibiting federal taxpayer funds from supporting non-citizen voting; also voted YES on the SAVE Act (H.R. 22, April 10, 2025) requiring citizenship proof for federal voter registration.
Consistent pro-gun voting record; backed bills reducing concealed-carry restrictions and has opposed assault-weapons bans and magazine-capacity limits throughout his congressional tenure.

Voted for H.R. 2, the Secure the Border Act of 2023 (House Vote #209, May 11, 2023), requiring DHS to resume border-wall construction and imposing stricter asylum standards; the bill passed 219-213 on a near-party-line vote. As a 14-term conservative from Virginia's Appalachian 9th district, Griffith maintains a consistent border-security and immigration-enforcement record, having also supported related measures requiring E-Verify and expanded ICE operational authority throughout the 118th and 119th Congresses.

A long-serving fiscal conservative who explicitly states he supports 'a real plan to balance our budget in Washington without raising taxes.' Griffith has consistently voted against large unbalanced spending packages and aligned with House Republican Conference efforts to pair any debt-ceiling increases with binding spending-reduction commitments. His 14-term record from coal-country Appalachian Virginia reflects sustained opposition to unconstrained federal borrowing and support for structural spending discipline.

Griffith voted for H.R. 3492, the Protect Children's Innocence Act of 2025 (House Vote #351, December 17, 2025, 216–211), which establishes federal criminal penalties for physicians who perform gender-transition surgeries or administer cross-sex hormones or puberty-blocking drugs to minors — affirming biological sex as fixed in federal law and rejecting gender-ideology medical interventions on children.

Griffith voted for H.R. 1919, the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act (119th Congress, passed the House 219–210 on July 17, 2025), which prohibits the Federal Reserve from issuing a retail central bank digital currency or maintaining individual financial accounts — opposing a programmable government digital dollar and defending decentralized money outside direct federal control.

claims.text: Committed to 'defending reproductive freedom' as a legislative priority since taking office in January 2025 and has taken a consistent pro-choice posture — rejecting any personhood-from-conception framework.

Introduced H.R. 9205 to expand the federal Gun Free School Zones Act and H.R. 9204 to create federal grants for safe-firearm-storage devices — both expanding federal gun restrictions the rubric opposes as infringements on Second Amendment rights.

Voted for the Laken Riley Act (January 2025), which mandates ICE detention of undocumented immigrants charged with theft or violent crimes — one of only 48 House Democrats to back the bill, signaling support for mandatory federal enforcement overriding local sanctuary policies.

A cosponsor of H.R. 15 (Equality Act, 119th Congress, introduced April 29, 2025), which would codify federal prohibitions on 'discrimination' on the basis of gender identity, requiring the government and covered entities to legally recognize transgender and non-binary self-identification as equal to biological sex — directly rejecting the male-female biological binary that the rubric's 'rejects transgender ideology' standard requires. His official office publishes a dedicated LGBTQ Resource Guide directing constituents to gender-affirmation services. Source: congress.gov / subramanyam.house.gov.

Voted Nay on H.R. 22 (SAVE Act, 119th Congress, Roll No. 102, April 10, 2025, passed 220-208), which requires documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. The 208 Nay votes were cast entirely by Democrats; Subramanyam was not among the four Democrats who crossed party lines to vote Yea (Golden-ME, Gluesenkamp Perez-WA, Cuellar-TX, Case-HI) — opposing the citizen-first election-integrity standard the rubric requires. Source: congress.gov / govtrack.us.

claims.text: As a sitting U.S. Representative for VA-11 since September 2025, championed legislation to limit restrictions on the provision of abortion services and protect providers' ability to offer reproductive services — rejecting any personhood protection from conception.

Publicly supports continued U.S. military aid to Ukraine and criticized President Trump's 'on-again, off-again support' for Ukraine as harmful to U.S. allies — opposing the rubric's preference for ending ongoing foreign military entanglements.

As a Fairfax County supervisor, Walkinshaw championed the county's Trust Policy — which limits local-government cooperation with federal immigration enforcement, barring officials from sharing immigration status information or detaining residents solely on ICE requests. In Congress, he publicly condemned Trump's immigration crackdowns as 'ICE terrorizing communities without warrants,' and his official immigration page states he supports 'comprehensive immigration reform to create a path to citizenship for the 14 million undocumented immigrants living in America' — a pro-sanctuary and anti-enforcement posture that directly opposes the rubric's anti-sanctuary standard.

On October 3, 2025, Walkinshaw cosponsored H.R. 3115, the Assault Weapons Ban of 2025, which would make it a federal crime to import, sell, manufacture, transfer, or possess a semiautomatic assault weapon or large-capacity ammunition feeding device — directly contradicting the rubric's opposition to assault-weapon bans and high-capacity magazine restrictions.

Walkinshaw cosponsored H.R. 14, the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act of 2025, which would reinstate and expand federal preclearance requirements under the Voting Rights Act, prioritizing expanded voting access and opposing state-level election integrity restrictions — at odds with the rubric's voter-ID, paper-ballot, and anti-mass-mail-in standards.

claims.text: Earle-Sears supports allowing abortion up to 15 weeks with exceptions for rape, incest, and threats to the pregnant woman's health — falling short of the rubric's life-at-conception personhood standard. This position permits on-demand abortion through the first trimester and rejects the principle that every unborn child has full legal personhood from the moment of fertilization.

When Earle-Sears was constitutionally required as Virginia's presiding lieutenant governor to sign a marriage-equality bill, she did so — but simultaneously added a written statement to the legislative record expressly declaring her personal and moral opposition to the bill's content, publicly reaffirming her conviction that marriage is the union of one man and one woman.

Opposing gender ideology in public schools was a prominent plank of Earle-Sears's 2025 Virginia gubernatorial campaign; she specifically targeted gender-ideology instruction in K-12 classrooms and pledged to protect children from ideologically driven curricula, aligning with the rubric's opposition to LGBTQ promotion in schools and public policy.

Wife Gina (married 30+ years); two children raised through Stafford schools. First elected Nov 2021 (sworn 1/1/2022); re-elected Nov 4 2025 in a 3-way race over Marcus Oats (D-endorsed I) and Kelly Robertson (I) 4,915-3,141-1,461; current term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029. candidates.name: Megan Jackson
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Livingston District since Jan 2024 (elected Nov 7 2023), term ends 12/31/2027 — NOT on Nov 2025 or Nov 2026 ballot. Chair since Jan 2025 (replaced Daniels 4-3), re-elected Chair Jan 2026. PARTY FIX: Unknown → D (real-world D-coalition; ballot nonpartisan but coalitions with Daniels/Cole/Medawar/Rodas reformer bloc that ousted the 2021-2023 Twigg/Taylor/Abuismail conservative majority; FXBG Advance 2025 election guide explicitly classifies the seat she holds as a 'pro-public-school candidate' seat). RELIGION + EDUCATION: not publicly disclosed — null. DEVIATION FLAGS: signed Nov 7 2025 no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from all SCPS property through July 1 2026; FIRE (Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression) opened a free-speech case against the board (fire.org); conservative outlet Spotsy Wire labels her 'Marxist Megan Jackson.' Voted YES on March 12 2024 firing of Christian-conservative Supt Mark Taylor (his federal lawsuit alleges retaliation for a Christian book fair). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted YES in 5-2 majority (Jackson, Daniels, Cole, Medawar, Rodas YES; Gillespie, Phelps NO) ROLLING BACK Twigg-era explicit-book-removal policy — new framework gives 'superintendent and principals' discretion rather than parent-driven removal. Rolling back parental-controls on K-12 library content is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes CRT, SOGI, comprehensive sex ed, and gender-ideology curricula in K-12 public schools'). HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0: voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor; Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 8 2024) alleges retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair promoting Christian literature. As Chair-elect at the time of the vote and the public face of the post-Twigg reformer majority, Jackson is on-record for the firing of a superintendent whose federal complaint alleges anti-Christian retaliation — direction FALSE on q0 ('right to publicly profess Christ in all spheres... schools'). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: presided as Chair over FY26 budget 5-0 adoption ($485.8M total, $170.8M local transfer, April 29 2025) — expansionary direction, opposite of the q2 oppose-deficit-spending axis. HARD FALSE self_defense q1: voted YES (5-0) Nov 2025 to RESTORE gun-free-zone designation for the SCPS administration building (paired with rollout of Evolv weapons-detection systems at all 5 high schools + admin building per WJLA / FFP 2025-11-11). Affirming a gun-free-zone designation is the rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, assault weapons bans, and gun registries' — adjacent direction on disarmament zones). SOFT FALSE public_justice q2: April 17 2025 — voted DOWN third-party HR audit after troubling hiring incidents in the division (FFP 2025-04-17). Voting down accountability infrastructure is opposite of the q2 direction ('supports victim restitution and proportional punishment of evildoers' — adjacent on institutional-accountability axis). SOFT FALSE christian_liberty q2: Nov 7 2025 signed no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from SCPS property through July 1 2026 after ejecting him from Oct 13 2025 meeting for 'racist rhetoric'; FIRE opened a free-speech case (fire.org). Jackson's own quote: 'The right to free speech is protected under the law, however, this right must be exercised in a manner that preserves the decorum...' Content-control on a hostile critic at public-comment is adjacent to the q2 compelled-speech direction (and FIRE's case framing is that the ban regulates protected expression at a public body). NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1, biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement), biblical_marriage q2 (Aug 2023 trans-policy vote PREDATES her Jan 2024 swearing-in — she cannot be scored on that vote), biblical_marriage q4 (no post-Jan-2024 Pride / pronoun / Title IX trans-bathroom vote located on her record), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice / homeschool statement located), family_child_sovereignty q1 (the opt-out provision adopted under her board provides PARTIAL parental notification — but the rubric direction is opt-in / strong consent for medical/gender interventions, and library content is not medical/gender-intervention; left null per discipline rather than scoring soft-FALSE on a non-clean fit), christian_liberty q3/q4 (no public-square-Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display votes located), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (SB office; N/A), public_justice q0/q1/q3/q4 (SRO program, bail reform, RICO, prosecutor oversight not on SB jurisdiction), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no COVID votes — pre-tenure; no ESG/equity-audit refusal — voted DOWN HR audit which is OPPOSITE direction; no state-curriculum-refusal votes), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A school office), self_defense q0 (no constitutional-carry advocacy located). Net 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% on cited — clean D-coalition / institutionalist post-Twigg-flip pattern across all five high-salience local rubric axes: voted UP book restoration, voted UP Supt-firing tied to Christian book fair lawsuit, voted UP expansionary budget, voted UP gun-free zone, voted DOWN HR audit, signed no-trespass order against critic. Recommend revisiting after the FY27 SB budget vote, the $237.2M bond referendum, any 2026-27 Title IX action, and the FIRE free-speech case resolution.

candidates.name: Dr. Lorita C. Daniels
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Salem District since Jan 2019 (elected 2018), re-elected 2023, term ends 12/31/2027. Chair Jan 2024 – Jan 2025. PARTY FIX: Unknown → D (HARD: filed for 2026 Democratic U.S. Senate primary challenging Sen. Warner). CANDIDACY STATUS: running_higher_office (sitting SB + 2026 D primary U.S. Senate candidate). OFFICE STRING augmented to flag dual-track status. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed in either SB or Senate campaign material — null. EDUCATION: Ph.D. Public Admin & Policy from Virginia Tech (the 'Dr.' prefix is legitimate, earned, not honorific) + MPA Valdosta State. DEVIATION FLAG: school-system-insider (Ph.D. in policy + adjunct prof + PTA + substitute teacher) on a board that flashpoint-flipped over parental-rights battles. COALITION: D-aligned reformer bloc (Daniels-Cole-Jackson-Medawar-Rodas) that ousted the 2021-23 Twigg-Taylor-Abuismail conservative majority. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: Aug 15 2023 Spotsy SB voted 4-2 (Shelley abstaining) to adopt the Youngkin VDOE model transgender policy requiring biological-sex bathrooms / pronouns / parental notification — Daniels voted NO, citing Title IX liability ('to make sure that we are clear what we're doing, because if we implement something that goes against Title IX, [that] is a liability'). Voting NO on biological-sex affirmation is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 (TRUE = rejects transgender ideology and affirms biological sex). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: same Aug 15 2023 NO vote on the Youngkin model policy is a vote AGAINST policy opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in schools, which is the q4 'opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy, schools' direction. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted YES (5-0) Jan 2024 to RESTORE 37 previously-removed books to school libraries (the Twigg-era removal had targeted sexually-explicit content); voted NO May 2023 on the 4-3 'sexually explicit content' definition added to book-challenge regulations (called it 'incomplete and misleading'); under her chairship the board rejected an opt-out form for library books in 2024. Rolling back parental-controls / content-restrictions on K-12 library content is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes CRT, SOGI, comprehensive sex ed, and gender-ideology curricula in K-12 public schools'). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q1: rejection of the library opt-out form weakens parental notification + consent on age-sensitive content (q1 'supports parental notification and consent on all... interventions for minors' direction). HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0: voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor for cause; Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 8 2024 per Fox 5 DC) alleges the firing was retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair promoting Christian literature. Daniels' own quote: 'We just needed to move this district forward'; she initially abstained, then changed her vote to YES in closed session ('I felt better about how we were moving forward with cause'). The board's stated cause was unlicensed hiring + FOIA violations + social-media conduct (not the book fair), but the Christian-expression nexus is in the public federal complaint, supporting a FALSE direction on christian_liberty q0 ('right to publicly profess Christ in all spheres... schools'). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on the FY25 Spotsy SB expansionary budget — total $479.9M, operating $418M (+$44M YoY) — in the 4-3 expansionary coalition vote (Daniels/Cole/Jackson/Medawar/Rodas YES); opposite direction from the rubric's oppose-deficit-spending / fiscal-restraint axis on q2. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion or sex-ed-curriculum statement; Senate campaign silent thus far), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement located), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice / homeschool-freedom statement on record — board has not voted on school-choice partnerships under her tenure), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4 (no compelled-speech / public-square-Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display votes located), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (SB office; declined), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (DEI quote at May 2025 Spotsy NAACP forum maps to family_child_sovereignty q2 direction not public_justice; no SRO / bail / prosecutor-oversight statement located; bullying-policy specifics not located), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (her Aug 2023 NO vote was DEFERENCE to federal Title IX, NOT local-control assertion against state mandates in the conservative direction — null on refuse_state_overreach rather than scoring backward), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A school office), self_defense q0/q1 (no armed-SRO / hardened-school / firearm-policy vote located on her record). Net 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% on cited — coherent D-aligned reformer-coalition pattern across all five high-salience local axes: voted DOWN biological-sex policy, voted UP book restoration, voted UP Supt-firing tied to Christian-expression lawsuit, voted UP expansionary budget. Recommend revisiting after the June 16 2026 D primary outcome (winner or loser, candidacy_status will shift), the FY27 SB budget vote, and any 2026 Senate-campaign positions on abortion / marriage / education that may surface.

Recommend revisiting after she casts her first FY27 budget vote, any Title IX policy votes, and the $237.2M bond referendum.

SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL RESTRAINT): voted NO on a recent SCPS budget citing fiscal-responsibility concerns about ~$2M in new bus-driver / bus expansion AND continued use of expiring (Sept 2024) ARPA pandemic-relief funds for operating expenses — rare fiscal-restraint footprint for otherwise progressive-aligned member. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion/sex-ed statement), biblical_marriage q0 (no direct marriage-definition statement; Pride displays defended in q4 instead), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT-specific vote — though Vlaming amicus is adjacent on q2-trans-pronoun-affirmation), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no religion disclosed; no invocation/Christmas/RFRA position; Vlaming amicus aligned AGAINST teacher conscience exemption but no direct christian_liberty vote separate from biblical_marriage q2), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no SB-applicable action), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A SB tier), self_defense q0/q1 (no statement), industry_capture q0/q3 (no statement), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no specific SRO/back-the-blue/charging/diversion position located beyond classroom-décor 'thin blue line flag' descriptive mention), refuse_state_overreach q0/q3/q4 (no other subsidiarity/local-control/nullification statement located). We will not go backward.' biblical_marriage q2: pledged to 'push back against any state or federal attempts to reduce protections and rights for LGBTQ+ people' — language treats LGBTQ+ rights as a unified bundle including T; left null per strict cite-only discipline (no specific quote on trans-identity / biological sex). profile.religion: Evangelical Christian — founding member of East End Fellowship, a multi-ethnic evangelical church in Church Hill, Richmond (NOT Catholic). Public bio at https://www.theologicalhorizons.org/events1/dannyavula confirms. Richmonder.org (campaign) flagged his 'Christian faith and affiliation with a church that's taken conservative stances on marriage and abortion' as a campaign issue — which Avula countered by publicly affirming LGBTQ equality and abortion rights.
profile.education: B.A. Biology, University of Virginia (1999); M.D., Virginia Commonwealth University; M.P.H., Johns Hopkins.
profile.background: Born 1978 in Hyderabad, India; immigrated as a child — first Asian-American and first immigrant Mayor of Richmond. Pediatrician by training. Career: Director, Richmond/Henrico Health Districts (6 yrs); Virginia State Vaccination Coordinator (Jan 2021 – early 2022, appointed by Gov. Northam); Commissioner, Virginia Department of Social Services (under Northam and Youngkin). Sworn in as 81st Mayor of Richmond Jan 1 2025; current term ends Jan 1 2029. Wife Mary Kay (RPS elementary teacher); five children including one adopted; openly gay daughter (featured in 2024 campaign ad). Self-described 'committed Democrat' on an officially-nonpartisan Richmond mayoral ballot.

candidates.name: Andrew Breton
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 1 (West End), 'Gumby' Breton, first term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Former chair of Richmond City Democratic Committee — real-world Democratic alignment on an officially-nonpartisan ballot. RPS public-school dad / former Mary Munford PTA fundraising chair (institutional ally of the public-school system). Most of his public posture is technocratic-progressive-urbanist (walkability, zoning reform / 'missing middle,' multimodal transit, RVAgreen 2050 climate plan, gas-utility phase-out, gun-safety) with DELIBERATE silence on culture-war markers (abortion, marriage, parental rights, religion). Per the SKILL's 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' discipline, the absence of cited quotes on those markers is captured as null rather than inferred FALSE. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: Richmond Mayorathon candidate Q&A — 'Funding resources and interventions for those who are most at-risk to commit violence will reduce incidents' is textbook public-health / intervention framing of violent crime; direction-adverse on q3. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting (bretonforrichmond.com/issues; Daily Progress 6/8/2023). Moms Demand Action is listed as a campaign endorser (bretonforrichmond.com/endorsements). Multi-vector pro-gun-control posture is direction-adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. Sept 8 2025 vote: supported activating the Civilian Review Board for RPD oversight, quote 'I'm glad to see that we can get the process started… many Richmonders will be glad to see that we're adding this additional layer of fair accountability to our justice system'; also said 'I believe it's very appropriate that we're also taking this moment to affirm Richmond's commitment to good policing now and moving forward' (WTVR 9/8/2025). NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0 — CRB activation is a process / accountability measure, not affirmative pro-police advocacy, and Breton's 'good policing' language is supportive-of-accountability framing not affirmative thin-blue-line; doesn't clear the HIGH BAR. NOT scored FALSE on q0 either — he didn't vote to defund and his 'commitment to good policing' framing is supportive enough to leave null per the strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored economic_stewardship q2 — supports a rebate / tax-relief approach ('we have room to roll back the tax rate' at richmonder.org candidate forum), but his specific yea/nay on the May 12-13 2025 FY26 budget 6-2 vote was not confirmed in retrieved sources; also backed an independent salary study for city employees and is mostly silent on aggressive rate cuts; left null per cite-only discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration — no quote-bearing positions located despite extensive search of campaign site, city council records, candidate questionnaires, and news coverage. Sparse 3 FALSE / 0 TRUE on cited cells reflects his deliberate culture-war silence in public materials — honest report per the 'NO EVIDENCE FOUND beats inference' brand discipline. Counter-archetype context: civic-review-board support + public-health framing of crime + Moms Demand endorsement establish a clear progressive-urbanist coalition profile, but the absence of any pro-life, pro-traditional-marriage, pro-school-choice, or pro-religious-liberty quotes also documents the deliberate silence — neither inference is fair. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, the June 10 2026 Pride proclamation cycle (will he sign or co-sponsor anything?), and any 2026 council action on sanctuary, abortion, or firearms.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of denominational affiliation or church membership. No public statement of faith in campaign materials, council bios, or news coverage. Wedding was at the Virginia Museum of Fine Arts — civil, not ecclesiastical — suggestive but not dispositive.
profile.education: B.A. University of Virginia (2004); graduate degree in economics, Duke University (2012).
profile.background: Born ~1982 in the D.C. area. Career: financial sector at BlackRock in New York City; left after the 2008 crisis ('witnessed banks bailed out while working families lost everything'); moved to Richmond 2008. Currently a financial data architect / data engineer. Wife is a physician (VCU Medical School); at least one child at Mary Munford Elementary (Breton served as PTA board member and fundraising chair). Prior civic work in Boston with Progressive Massachusetts (chapter chair, statewide board). Former CHAIR of the Richmond City Democratic Committee before running for council. Organized the June 7 2023 children's march on the Virginia State Capitol for gun-safety legislation following the Huguenot HS graduation shooting. Richmond City Council, District 1 (West End), officially-nonpartisan ballot, sworn 1/1/2025; term 1/1/2025 – 12/31/2028. Won Nov 5 2024 general 49.1% defeating Paul Goldman 27.7% and Zachary Walker 22.4%, succeeding Andreas Addison (who ran for mayor).

Affirmative Pride proclamation, the cleanest hit on q4 ('opposes city/county Pride proclamations' inverse). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the Nov 12 2024 proposed real estate tax rate reduction from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (vpm.org 11/12/2024 + richmondfreepress.com 11/14/2024); in 2025 backed Avula's plan to keep the rate the same when the cut was proposed again; as VP whipped the council on the FY26 $3B budget with the 'We are in this together as a city' line (richmondbizsense.com 5/13/2025). HARD FALSE public_justice q3: 2020 Richmond Magazine Q&A framed gun violence and homelessness around 'upstream issues' like living wages and wraparound services rather than emergency responses; supports a social-worker mental-health-call response model that displaces police on mental-health calls — explicit root-causes / public-health framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3). candidates.name: Ellen Robertson
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 6 (Gateway), serving continuously since Jan 2004 (sworn after Nov 2003 election); re-elected Nov 5 2024 53.3% three-way race; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on Nov 12 2024 Trammell-proposed real estate tax rate cut from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 5-4; Robertson with Trammell/Lambert/Lynch in the YES minority); actively pressured the Avula administration in committee for a lower-rate baseline budget — quoted asking CAO Odie Donald II whether the administration would build the budget around a lower rate if Council asked; Donald replied 'No ma'am' and Robertson pushed back suggesting $16M in prior-year surplus could absorb the $3.2M revenue hit. Heterodox-D fiscal-restraint signal — the cleanest cross-party catch this batch (parallel to Trammell/Lambert/Lynch faction against the Newbille/Avula hold-the-rate coalition). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: Mayor Levar Stoney publicly credited Robertson (with then-Councilmember Parker Agelasto) as 'key in moving Richmond's Human Rights Commission and non-discrimination laws forward in the city council,' which produced the city's SOGI non-discrimination framework AND 'transgender-inclusive health benefits for city employees' that lifted Richmond's HRC Municipal Equality Index score 52 points (chpn.net 2018) — affirmative advocacy on the trans-affirming-policy-for-city-employees direction, the rubric's adverse direction on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: voted with then-Councilmember Mike Jones in committee 2-1 to advance the 2020 police-defunding study resolution to full council with a recommendation to approve (wric.com); founded Richmond's Office of Community Wealth Building (root-causes-of-poverty framing); chairs the city's Alternatives to Incarceration Board; on the Northam marijuana-legalization-amendments advance she framed legalization as supporting 'people who are incarcerated for possession' and 'help[ing] people seek health assistance' (wric.com) — explicit decarceral / soft-on-crime direction across multiple vectors. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: in her own voice as Council Vice President, May 13 2021 gun-violence-prevention rally — 'Gun violence is certainly a public crisis that extends beyond Richmond; it's a national crisis that calls for us to use all the resources at our disposal to keep people safe' (rva.gov) — textbook public-health/national-crisis framing of violent crime, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 (parallel to Doyle 'rec centers / libraries / community groups' framing scored FALSE in the 1550 Norfolk batch). NOT scored biblical_marriage q4 (Pride proclamations): research confirms Robertson was NOT a signatory on the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community (Avula + Newbille + Jordan signed; Robertson did not) — null not FALSE per strict cite-only discipline (absence of signature is not affirmative opposition; she also has not publicly dissented). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Robertson said 'Richmond needs a police department. I am open to exploring concerns and addressing them' on the 2020 defund proposal but did not make an affirmative pro-police defense of the chief or oppose-defund advocacy; her actual committee vote ADVANCED the defund study. Null on q0 + the FALSE on q1 captures the soft-on-crime posture without overreach. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / industry_capture / public_justice q2 + q4 — no quote-bearing positions located in 22 years of service despite the long council tenure (sparse cultural-issue record on a fiscally-mixed urban progressive). 1 TRUE / 3 FALSE on cited cells = 25% — coherent center-left urban D council member whose distinctive feature is heterodox fiscal restraint (Trammell-coalition tax-cut yes-vote + active administration pressure for a lower-rate budget). Recommend revisiting if Robertson signs a future Pride proclamation, takes a position on the 2025 Avula sanctuary stance, or after the FY27 budget cycle.

Re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 53.3% (vs Willie Hilliard Jr. 31.6% and former aide Tavares Floyd 14.4%); current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot, next election 2028. Has previously served as Council Vice President. Resident of the North Highland Park neighborhood.

candidates.name: Cynthia Newbille
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 7 (East End), current term 1/2/2025-12/31/2028, currently Council President 2025-2026 (3rd stint). Officially nonpartisan; real-world Democratic alignment. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (CLEARS HIGH BAR via multi-vector affirmative pro-police record): (1) July 2020 amid George Floyd unrest she voted with majority REJECTING reductions to the RPD budget in response to defund-the-police demands (Richmond Free Press 7/30/2020, WRIC) — 'conditional no' on defund framework; (2) 2022 affirmatively proposed $1.9M-$4M in RPD and FIRE pay raises with VP Robertson and Trammell (multi-source) — that is advocacy, not routine governance; (3) Feb 2026 walked Shockoe Bottom businesses with Police Chief Rick Edwards after a mass shooting, quoted 'The goal is to share, to hear from the community, and to really map out strategies' — partnership/community-policing work beyond budget. Three independent vectors of affirmative pro-police advocacy clears the SKILL's HIGH BAR ('NOT just routine collaboration… affirmative advocacy: runs a police-partnership program, public pro-police statements'). This is a meaningful counter-archetype catch — a Democratic-aligned council president with a real pro-police record. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: as Council President co-signed the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation 'Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' with Mayor Avula and Council VP Jordan (rva.gov). Affirmative state-government LGBTQ promotion = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: Oct 14-15 2025 co-sponsored with Mayor Avula the proposal to HOLD the real-estate tax rate steady at $1.20/$100, defeating Reva Trammell's proposed cut; direct Newbille quote: 'I look forward to when we will be able to reduce the tax rate. I do not see that in this moment.' (VPM 10/15/2025). Direct on-record opposition to tax-rate cut → adverse on rate-restraint cell. Voted YES on May 13 2025 6-2 adoption of the $3B FY26 budget keeping the $1.20 rate. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: May 24 2021 voted with unanimous council to declare gun violence a 'public health crisis' alongside Mayor Stoney (WTVR 5/13/2021, WRIC, RVA.gov GVPI page). Given her public-health PhD and decades of public-health-policy career, this is the most quintessential q3 fit on the council. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: Sept 13 2021 voted with unanimous council to pass the Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution and endorsed creation of a residential energy-efficiency program (Sierra Club Falls of the James, Richmonder energy-efficiency story) — the rubric's q2 cell specifically names ESG, and unanimous-council ESG-aligned action with her vote = direction-adverse. HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: as Council President co-signed admin statement 'Local government is not in charge of federal immigration law enforcement and Richmond does not detain or arrest anyone due to immigration status' (RVA.gov immigration response page); RPD under city policy has not signed a 287(g) agreement; Richmond appears/appeared on the federal sanctuary-jurisdictions list; in July 2025 when a federal agent accessed RPD Flock Safety camera data for immigration enforcement Newbille stayed publicly silent despite multiple media inquiries (Church Hill Lookout — HARD documentation of non-response). Anti-ICE-cooperation posture = adverse on q2's federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: July 1 2019 voted YES on the ordinance banning firearms in Richmond city buildings and parks; direct Newbille quote characterized it as 'one way to limit gun violence' and said council 'needed to act to help citizens feel safe on city property' (WTVR 7/1/2019); supported (with delay for refinement) Stoney's ordinance prohibiting firearms near permitted events. Richmond did NOT pass a Second Amendment Sanctuary resolution under her council presidency, in contrast to most VA rural localities in the 2019-2020 wave — directionally adverse to the constitutional-carry / oppose-restrictions cells. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice q1/q2/q4 / refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q3/q4 / border_immigration q1 — no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — Pride proclamation cite scores q4 (LGBTQ promotion) directly but no marriage-definition or biological-sex quote → null per strict cite-only discipline. Counter-archetype context (CAPTURED in q0 TRUE): she is NOT a defunder. Her July 2020 vote with the majority rejecting police-budget cuts + her 2022 pay-raise proposal + her partnership work with Chief Edwards are real and meaningful softeners on the standard Democratic-progressive coding. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote and any 2026 ICE-policy or 2A council action.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of specific congregation or denominational affiliation. Richmond Magazine 'Flip to the Back' profile records personal piety quotes ('When all else fails, I pray, I plan, I proceed' and reads religious literature) — SOFT evidence of personal Christian practice without a publicly-identifiable church home.
profile.education: B.A. psychology/linguistics, SUNY Stony Brook (1974); M.A. psychology, SUNY Stony Brook (1983); Ph.D. Public Policy/Administration with Health Policy concentration, Virginia Commonwealth University (2010).
profile.background: Raised in Whitcomb Court public housing in the East End; Whitcomb Elementary, Mosby Middle, Armstrong High School. Public-health / nonprofit career: Executive Director, National Black Women's Health Project (Atlanta, early 1990s); Head Start Program director at Charles Drew University (Compton, CA); Interim Co-director, Richmond City Public Health Department; Associate Director, VCU Grace E. Harris Leadership Institute; Senior Advisor, Richmond Memorial Health Foundation; Annie E. Casey Foundation consultant; Chief of Staff to Richmond City Manager. Co-founder of SisterFund (2015), giving circle for African-American women/girls. Sports Backers board member. Vegetarian. Lives near 27th & Nine Mile Road, East End. Richmond City Council, District 7 (East End), officially-nonpartisan (real-world Democratic alignment). First elected via special election 2009; current term Jan 2 2025 – Dec 31 2028 (re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 66.46% over Eric Sundberg's 32.83%). Council President 2019-2022, Vice President 2017-2018, Council President again 2025-2026 (third stint).

HARD TRUE sanctity_of_life q1: that 2022 refusal is the cleanest cross-party signal on the rubric's sanctuary-city-for-abortion inverse direction. NOT scored biblical_marriage cells: no public Trammell-individual record on Pride proclamations (Richmond's joint proclamations have been Mayor + Council Pres + VP signatories, not full council), no public position on drag-in-libraries / trans / SOGI located — null per discipline. candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council South Central 9th Voter District, appointed interim 12/12/2023 (sworn 1/1/2024), elected to full term Nov 5 2024 w/ 58%, current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Lamont Bagby (D), multiple RPS School Board members, predecessor Dr. Michael J. Jones (D, now HD-77), and listed in 2024 Progressive Voters Guide; also endorsed by Richmond Association of Realtors, Richmond Building Trades, Home Building Association of Richmond. No GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. candidates.name: Colette McEachin
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Commonwealth's Attorney, City of Richmond, VA (D); new term seated Jan 2026 after winning 2025 Dem primary 71% and running unopposed Nov 2025 general. EDUCATION CORRECTION: Brown undergrad + UVA Law (not W&M / Anglican as I speculated). RELIGION CORRECTION: institutionally Baptist (St. Peter Baptist CDC board), NOT AME (her late husband was the AME minister, not her). SOROS-DA FINDING: NO evidence she received Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC money — that money flowed to her 2025 challenger Tom Barbour. She publicly declined to join VA Progressive Prosecutors for Justice and was attacked from her left in the primary. Heritage Foundation / LELDF Soros-DA rosters do NOT list her. HARD TRUE election_integrity q4: Oct 6 2025 her office personally announced a 4-count Class-5 felony election-fraud indictment of 2024 Richmond City Council candidate Tavares Floyd after a 10-month Virginia State Police probe (campaign-finance falsification, dead 'donors') — affirmative willingness to prosecute election crime. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: continued Herring's policy of not seeking cash bail in many cases — 'virtually eliminated the cash bond system for those awaiting trial' (Virginia Mercury); diversion programs for first-time non-violent, addiction, mental-health, behavioral-health; created a 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime'; expanded expungement. Multi-vector bail-reform / soft-on-crime trigger per the rubric definition (even though she also prosecutes murders aggressively and explicitly declined the 'progressive prosecutor' label — net cited direction is FALSE on q1). HARD FALSE public_justice q3: same 'Community Justice Reform Unit to tackle root causes of crime' + Behavioral Health Docket + restorative-justice integration = public-health framing of crime (rubric-adverse). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1 + q2: office statement 'We do not work with any local, state or federal law enforcement agency to detain or arrest anyone based solely upon their immigration status'; McEachin direct quote 'ICE cannot just barge into the Richmond Circuit Court courthouse, or any of the courthouses, and just grab people'; personally distributes 'blue cards' to immigrant communities in English/Spanish explaining rights to refuse searches and access counsel; office denied FOIA request for ICE communications; her quote 'This is me saying to Trump, the Constitution still lives.' Clear anti-ICE-cooperation posture on the federal-preemption-against-sanctuary cell. NOT scored TRUE on public_justice q0: 2020 Marcus-David Peters JUSTIFIED ruling (refused to reopen despite intense activist pressure) and 2020 RPD-officer charge handling (dismissed misdemeanor assault charges after restorative-justice resolution) are evidence she is NOT a Soros-style anti-cop prosecutor — but they are prosecutorial / judicial determinations based on evidence, NOT 'affirmative advocacy' per the SKILL's HIGH BAR (no FOP endorsement, no anti-defund rally, no police-partnership program she runs). Per skill discipline q0 left null rather than sparse-data-trap TRUE. NOT scored q2 (victim restitution): campaign touts 'expanded services supporting victims of crime' and family-justice-center goal but those are victim-services language, not specifically defendant-pays-victim restitution; left null per strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored q4 (Romans 13:4 magistrate sword): her 'sacred duty' framing ('We are the scales of justice... that is a sacred duty') is generic civic-religious not Christian-explicit; left null. NOT scored self_defense q0/q1: her aggressive gun-violence prosecution ('if you use a gun in a harmful or threatening way in the city of Richmond, you will be prosecuted') and 'you are making Richmond dangerous because you have a gun' are anti-gun rhetoric but do not specifically map to the rubric's constitutional-carry / red-flag / AWB / registry cells; left null. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / economic_stewardship / refuse_state_overreach — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 5 FALSE on cited cells = ~17% — coherent for a center-left D prosecutor who has implemented some reformist policies (bail, restorative justice) while still aggressively prosecuting homicides, gun violence, and election fraud. Recommend revisiting on the 2026 prosecution-priorities report and any next-term policy announcements.

profile.religion: Institutionally Baptist — public bio states she previously served on the governing board of St. Peter Baptist Church Child Development Center. No public personal profession of faith in office located. Distinct from her late husband Rep. A. Donald McEachin, who was an ordained AME minister.
profile.education: B.A. Brown University; J.D. University of Virginia School of Law (per primary bio at rva.gov/commonwealths-attorney/about — note: prior brief speculation 'William & Mary undergrad' is incorrect).
profile.background: First woman and first African-American woman elected Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Richmond. 25+ years in the Richmond CA's office before winning the 2019 special general election after predecessor Mike Herring resigned to join McGuireWoods (May 2019). Re-elected unopposed in 2021. Won the June 17 2025 Democratic primary over Tom Barbour Jr. (~71% vs ~29%) and ran unopposed in the Nov 4 2025 general; new term seated Jan 2026. Widow of the late Rep. A. Donald McEachin (VA-04), who died Nov 2022; they met in law school at UVA and have three children. Life member Delta Sigma Theta, NAACP, Richmond Crusade for Voters. Notable: she publicly DECLINED to join 'Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice,' saying she 'doesn't need to join a club' to make change — her 2025 primary challenger Tom Barbour ran from her left as the explicitly-progressive challenger, asserting 'Colette McEachin is not a progressive prosecutor' (Virginia Mercury, VPM).

candidates.name: Matthew Percival
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools School Board Vice Chair, District 1, sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Fix party Unknown→I (formally nonpartisan ballot; no public D/R registration confirmed). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed; symbolic Jan 8 2025 oath taken with NO BOOK (richmonder.org). EDUCATION/CAREER: U.S. State Department information-security engineer + 10+ years RPS PTA. COALITION SIGNALS: REGS-endorsed (REGS is a governance/equity-focused PAC critics accused of being a charter-school advocacy front; REGS denies); personally supported by outgoing progressive District 1 member Elizabeth Doerr (who sponsored the 2022 anti-Youngkin trans resolution); unanimous Vice Chair pick of the new 2024-elected majority. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the March 2026 motion to add $3M Virtual Academy funding to the RPS budget proposal (motion passed 6-2 over his minority position; quoted 'An additional $3 million may be a difficult ask in this environment,' vpm.org 3/3/2026); voted YES on the June 2026 unanimous ~$4M FY27 RPS budget deficit cuts (incl. teacher-stipend cuts + closing the Virtual Academy, vpm.org 6/3/2026); separately charter-skeptical per Richmonder quote — needs 'to be convinced that data supports the approach of having a charter school in Richmond right now' and 'opening a new school that competes for dollars and teachers is not the first priority' (richmonder.org). Multi-cycle fiscal-restraint signal — direct vote against budget growth + active deficit-closing. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: campaign platform explicitly opposes parental/governmental review of library content — teachers should not be 'search[ed for] subversive messages' in their classrooms and should have 'freedom to use age-appropriate literature they believe will best prepare students for life beyond graduation' and 'impart truthful lessons without fear of being fired' (vpm.org elections 2024-11-01 Q&A). Explicit anti-parental-rights / anti-book-review framing — the inverse of family q2 ('school board has voted to remove CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials from district curriculum and library shelves'). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1: voted YES (unanimous board, Feb 4 2025) for the RPS 'Safe Zone' resolution declaring ICE presence on campuses 'substantially disrupts the learning environment' and limiting agent admission to cases where law requires it; RPS was first-in-area to adopt such policy (axios.com 2/7/2025 + vpm.org 2/5/2025). As Vice Chair he was part of the unanimous coalition. Direct sanctuary-style anti-ICE-cooperation vote — rubric-adverse on q1 ('cooperates with ICE detainers + transfers' inverse). HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: same Feb 4 2025 Safe Zone vote also maps to q2 ('voted AGAINST local sanctuary-city ordinances' inverse) since the RPS resolution functions as a school-grounds sanctuary policy. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q3: April 2025 RPS budget discussion — Percival expressed concern about RPS being trapped on 'Comprehensive Support and Improvement' status IF Trump dismantles the U.S. Department of Education (vpm.org 4/9/2025); his posture is generally pro-federal-DoE-oversight, NOT subsidiarity / lowest-competent-level. Direct anti-subsidiarity quote (rubric-adverse on q3 'Official affirms subsidiarity'). NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q0 (school choice / charters / ESAs): charter-skeptical per Richmonder quote suggests FALSE direction but no actual vote against a charter application during his tenure to anchor it; null per discipline absent vote. NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1: no specific parental notification vote located during his ~17 mo tenure. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): no SRO defense quote located either direction. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / christian_liberty / election_integrity (not applicable at school board) / public_justice q1-q4 / refuse_overreach q0+q1+q2+q4 / self_defense / industry_capture — no quote-bearing positions located. NOTABLE QUOTES: at Jan 8 2025 organizational meeting — 'the real story here tonight is consensus and collaboration'; on collective bargaining — compared changes to 'a one-size-fits-all sweater not designed to flatter anybody but to keep everyone warm'; campaign joke / platform — 'Make School Board Boring Again' buttons. 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 20% — coherent moderate-progressive consensus / governance-focused first-term Vice Chair w/ State Department IT background, fiscally restrained but on all culture-war markers aligned with the prevailing progressive-RPS-coalition direction. Recommend revisiting after FY28 budget cycle and any 2027 curriculum / library-content votes.

candidates.name: Anne Holton
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan, real-world firm Democratic alignment — former VA SecEd under McAuliffe, wife of Sen. Tim Kaine, daughter of moderate-R Gov. Linwood Holton but her own career is firmly Democratic). Interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by 7-0-1 board vote to fill the Shonda Harris-Muhammed seat; running for the seat in Nov 2026 special. RELIGION CORRECTION: Catholic at St. Elizabeth Richmond (not Episcopalian as I speculated). EDUCATION CORRECTION: Princeton + Harvard Law (not W&M / UVA). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q0: as VA Secretary of Education 2014-2016 'pushed back against the expansion of charter schools' in Virginia and advocated for teacher pay increases instead (WVTF Jul 28 2016, Wash Post); on VA Board of Education Apr 25 2024 was the lone NO vote on the Roanoke College lab school program and four others, direct quote: 'I'm concerned that, in our haste to get this done quickly, we're lowering the standards for what we've seen in the rest of lab schools, just to get it done fast.' HARD multi-vector evidence of opposition to school-choice expansion. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: on VA BOE 2022-2023 objected to Youngkin's draft history standards, called the November 2022 draft 'a disaster,' noted Reagan-5x-vs-Obama-0x asymmetry, said 'Teachers have to walk gingerly around anything that connects what happens today with the past'; joined Board President Daniel Gecker and VP Tammy Mann (Northam/McAuliffe appointees) opposing the introductory pages as 'political' (Virginia Mercury, WDBJ7). HARD-direction adverse on the CRT / standards-restriction rubric cell. SOFT FALSE biblical_marriage q4 + family_child_sovereignty q1 + refuse_state_overreach q0: Aug 29 2024 X thread (status 1829241205441311103) + Blue Virginia called the Youngkin BOE's 'changes' — which centered on parental notification, biological-sex / model-policy reversal, and conservative history standards — 'terrible changes that are bad for VA students, parents, and educators' (HARD direction-adverse statement on the policies the cited cells protect). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: Mar 2-3 2026 RPS FY27 budget Holton and VP Matthew Percival cast the 2 NO votes in the 6-2 board approval of the ~$549.9M Avula-proposed package, arguing it was 'unrealistic' to expect additional funds with city property-tax freezes coming (VPM Mar 3 2026, Axios Richmond Mar 2 2026); also voted against funding the Richmond Virtual Academy for early-cut reasons. Affirmative fiscal-restraint vote against an oversized education budget = rubric-positive on q2 even from a Democratic education-establishment figure (a meaningful counter-archetype softener captured per skill discipline). HARD FALSE self_defense q0 + q1: campaigns on 'tighter restrictions on guns,' cites 'over 50 [RPS] students [lost] to guns' in seven years and calls the situation 'inexcusable' (The Richmonder special-election article). NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty / election_integrity / public_justice / border_immigration — outside school-board lane and no quote-bearing positions located. NOT scored biblical_marriage q0/q2 — RPS Oct 3 2022 transgender policy rejection vote (8-1) PREDATES her tenure (she joined Feb 2026), no direct vote record; her general posture is direction-adverse but rubric strictness keeps q0/q2 null and credits q4 only because the Aug 2024 X thread specifically named the policy reversal she opposed. NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1 cited cell despite Aug 2024 thread — promoted from null to SOFT-FALSE based on the same cite because the Youngkin model policies center on parental notification on social transition specifically. NOT scored refuse_state_overreach q1-q4: no specific quotes on COVID-mandate-refusal / ESG / subsidiarity / refused-enforcement located. 1 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = ~14% — coherent for standard Northam/McAuliffe-Democratic education establishment figure with a fiscal-caution counter-archetype softener (the FY27 NO vote). Recommend revisiting after the Nov 2026 special election outcome and any 2027 RPS culture-war votes.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — congregant of St. Elizabeth Catholic Church in Richmond, a predominantly African American parish; married Tim Kaine in November 1984 at a Roman Catholic church in Richmond (per Wikipedia, primary biography sources).
profile.education: Princeton University, Woodrow Wilson School of Public and International Affairs, magna cum laude (1980); J.D. Harvard Law School (1983).
profile.background: Born Feb 1 1958 in Roanoke, VA. Daughter of moderate-Republican Gov. Linwood Holton (1970-1974) — at age 12 her father enrolled her at the predominantly Black Mosby Middle School (now MLK Jr. Middle School) to end Massive Resistance to school integration, a defining biographical fact she cites constantly. Career: law clerk for Judge Robert R. Merhige Jr. (EDVA); Central Virginia Legal Aid Society attorney (1983-1998); Richmond Juvenile & Domestic Relations District Court judge (sworn 6/30/1998, chief judge 2000-2003, resigned late 2005); First Lady of VA 2006-2010 (married Sen. Tim Kaine); Virginia Secretary of Education 1/11/2014 – 7/25/2016 under Gov. Terry McAuliffe; Virginia Board of Education member Feb 2017 – ~2024 (appointed by McAuliffe); George Mason University visiting professor (May 2017) and interim president 6/20/2019 – 6/30/2020. Three adult children — Nat (b.1990), Woody (b.1992), Annella (b.1995) — all RPS graduates. Brothers Woody Holton (historian, USC) and Dwight Holton (former US Attorney, Oregon). RPS School Board, District 6 (officially nonpartisan ballot, real-world Democratic alignment) — interim-appointed Feb 3 2026 by a 7-0-1 board vote to fill the seat vacated by resigned Shonda Harris-Muhammed (Dec 2025, departed to be Southampton County superintendent); announced she will run in the Nov 2026 special election (candidacy_status set general_candidate).

candidates.name: Kenneth Cooper 'Kenny' Alexander
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Norfolk (officially nonpartisan; D-aligned per lifelong party affiliation + 2024 Harris-Walz Norfolk LGBTQ rally appearance). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: Direct quote in WTKR coverage of gun-violence response — 'My council and I, we are not defunding police. We are funding police.' Plus 13NewsNow (June 2020) reporting Alexander 'do[es] not support reducing the police department's budget at all.' State of the City messaging across 2024-2026 credits adding 43 new sworn officers + 'largest academy class of sheriff's deputies in a decade' for the 20% violent-crime drop. Multi-year affirmative pro-police pattern clears the high bar — this is exactly the kind of D-aligned official who EARNS the TRUE on the police-backing cell per the rubric's principle of giving credit where earned. HARD adverse christian_liberty q0 evidence: in WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile, Alexander explicitly framed public profession of his Christian conviction as off-limits in governance — 'You have to be careful that you understand that line, and never cross that line, because then you are no better than the Taliban.' He is a Christian minister (licensed and ordained since the 1980s, nondenominational) who deliberately keeps Christianity out of policy; that is documented evidence AGAINST the rubric's affirmative-public-profession standard. SOFT adverse biblical_marriage q4 evidence: WHRO 9/4/2024 — Alexander gave remarks endorsing Harris-Walz at 'Out for Harris' LGBTQ-community rally at MJ's Tavern in Norfolk. Explicit alliance with LGBTQ political activism. (Not scored as HARD because I did not locate a signed Pride proclamation from the Mayor's office — the City's Pride Month communications appear to be administrative/PR, not mayoral proclamations.) NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement, no PP endorsement, no SBA endorsement), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — mayor doesn't sit on NPS school board; no comment located on the 2023 NPS 'Get Real' adoption or any school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI issue), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4, economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — local budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (his state-senate roll calls on SB 731/SB 719 not retrievable in this pass), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4 (no documented friction with Richmond on lockdowns/mandates/ESG/unconstitutional directives — generally cooperative posture), border_immigration q1/q2 (no statement; Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron operates an ICE IGSA at the city jail but that is sheriff function, not mayoral), self_defense q0/q1 (NO Norfolk firearms-in-buildings ordinance on the books — the 2020 ordinance was pulled by the City Attorney before any roll-call and never returned; cannot score Alexander adverse on that basis). Counter-archetype context: D mayor + ordained Christian minister + affirmative anti-defund pro-police record + explicit church-state separation posture. Verification note: the 'Apostolic bishop' framing sometimes used did NOT verify — he is listed as 'licensed and ordained Minister' per WTKR; not on Norfolk Apostolic Church staff (Sr. Pastor is Michael Blankenship).

profile.religion: Christian (licensed and ordained Minister since the 1980s, nondenominational, non-pastoring; speaks at 'churches, temples, mosques, and synagogues' per WTKR 2017 'The Mayor's Ministry' profile). Explicitly compartmentalizes faith from governance.
profile.education: Lake Taylor HS (1985); A.S. Mortuary Science (Brightpoint CC, formerly John Tyler CC); B.A. Political Science (Old Dominion); M.A. Diplomacy (Norwich); Ph.D. Leadership and Change (Antioch, 2019)
profile.background: Born and raised in Norfolk. President, Metropolitan Funeral Service. Vice Chancellor for Strategic Partnerships, Virginia Community College System. VA House of Delegates 89th District 2002-2012. VA Senate 5th District 2012-2016. Virginia Legislative Black Caucus chair (2008). 25th and first Black Mayor of Norfolk (since 7/1/2016); re-elected 11/5/2024 with 59.3% (def. Thomas Leeman Jr. 33.4%, Giovanni Dolmo 6.6%). Current term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028. Norfolk City Council mayoral seat is officially nonpartisan but Alexander is a lifelong Democrat; endorsed Harris-Walz at a Norfolk 'Out for Harris' rally Sept 2024.

candidates.name: Martin A. Thomas Jr.
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Council Ward 1 since Aug 2016 special election (re-elected 2022, term ends 12/31/2026); Vice Mayor since July 2018 (re-elected Jan 2023). RELIGION: Catholic by inference from St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board service 2010-2017 (not self-declared in public statements). EDUCATION: VT public-affairs + Richmond Law. REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: Democratic — appointed to VA College Building Authority by Gov. Northam (D), endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90, appeared in McGee endorsement coalition alongside Mayor Alexander (D) and Sens. Rouse + Williams Graves; no GOP affiliation. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14 2024 FY25 Norfolk budget that CUT real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): law-firm professional bio (https://petedecker.com/martin-thomas-jr/) explicitly states Thomas 'has successfully championed increases in pay for public safety employees' — direct affirmative-advocacy self-description; the corresponding Norfolk FY25 budget Thomas voted for delivered a 10%+ raise for nearly 300 police officers (https://www.wavy.com/news/local-news/norfolk/norfolk-approves-budget-with-a-more-than-10-raise-for-nearly-300-police-officers-750m-school-construction-plan/). Direct quote on his policy stance (campaign-bio): Norfolk 'must have a world-class police force' and the city 'needs to pay public safety officers well and create a culture and environment where they can thrive.' Multi-vector pro-police: self-stated championship + corresponding budget vote + first-tier policy quote = clears HIGH BAR. NOT scored christian_liberty q4: agent flagged Dec 12 2023 Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 yes-vote but (a) Thomas issued no individual quote, (b) LOCAL q4 specifically reads 'defends churches from zoning attacks' not generic free-speech defense, so the vote does not map to q4; left null per strict cite-only discipline. NOT scored public_justice q3: agent considered FALSE on PD Chief Boone's holistic-framing budget posture but no Thomas-specific quote located; left null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty other cells / election_integrity / public_justice q1-q4 / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions or council votes located (Norfolk simply did not take up Pride flag-raising votes, sanctuary votes, 287(g) votes, 2A sanctuary votes, or guns-in-public-spaces votes in his tenure that surfaced — the 2020 GOP 2A-sanctuary petition fell ~179 signatures short and the 2020 guns-in-public ordinance was pulled before vote). 2 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells = 100% — sparse-data trap acknowledged but both TRUEs are HARD (FY25 rate-cut vote + multi-vector affirmative pro-police record). This is a textbook 'Democrat-aligned but conservative-direction on fiscal + police' counter-archetype softener and the public_justice q0 high-bar clearance is the genuine cross-track catch. Recommend revisiting after Nov 2026 re-election filing confirmation and FY27 budget cycle.

profile.background: Partner since 2006 at Decker, Cardon, Thomas, Weintraub & Neskis PC (Decker Law) — personal-injury, criminal, traffic, civil litigation practice. Won the Aug 23 2016 Norfolk City Council Ward 1 special election; re-elected Nov 8 2022 (79.3% vs Padraig-Eoin Leigh Dalrymple's 19.7%); current term Jan 1 2023 - Dec 31 2026. Elected Vice Mayor by council July 2018; re-elected Vice Mayor by unanimous roll-call at Jan 2023 reorganization meeting (term coterminous with Ward 1 seat). Civic record includes the Virginia College Building Authority (appointed by Gov. Ralph Northam, D) and the St. Mary's Catholic Cemetery Board (2010-2017). Wife Maggie, two sons; Wards Corner area Norfolk resident. Officially nonpartisan ballot — real-world Democratic alignment (Northam appointment + endorsed Democrat Angelia Williams Graves for VA HD-90 + appeared at McGee endorsement event with Mayor Alexander).

RELIGION: Catholic at Sacred Heart Norfolk (per campaign about page). EDUCATION: UMD undergrad + ODU MBA. CONFIRMED DEM-aligned despite I-nonpartisan ballot — Progressive Voters Guide endorsed her 2022 with note 'support for pandemic safety' + climate-leadership credit; ballot remains officially nonpartisan. Norfolk runs an annual city Pride Month program with LGBTQ Community Liaison office; Doyle has not publicly dissented from any Pride proclamation in seven years of council service. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on May 14, 2024 FY25 budget that CUT the real estate tax rate 2¢ (from $1.25 to $1.23 per $100) + 51% personal-property tax relief on vehicles $1k-$20k + 100% relief on vehicles ≤$1k. FY26 budget (May 12, 2026) maintained the $1.23 rate, vote 7-1, she was on the YES majority. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: campaign Safe Neighborhoods page (https://courtneydoyle.com/safe-neighborhoods/) describes her approach to gun violence as 'using the public education system, recreation centers, libraries, and community groups to impress upon youth that guns are not the solution' — textbook root-causes / public-health framing (adverse on q3). HARD FALSE self_defense q1: same page advocates for 'constructive and meaningful gun legislation that prevents guns from getting into the hands of those who shouldn't possess a gun' — affirmative gun-control framing (adverse on q1's red-flag / AWB / registry direction). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2: signature platform issue is climate / sea-level-rise leadership ('positioned our city as a leader in combating climate challenges,' campaign flooding page; Progressive Voters Guide credit on 'climate change safety measures' is endorsement-bearing evidence on the ESG-embrace direction). NOT scored christian_liberty q0: she voted YES on Armed Forces Brewing Co. 6-1 explicitly on content-neutral free-speech grounds (her direct quote: 'I took an oath to uphold the right to free speech despite the fact I disagree with the speech'), but LOCAL q0 specifically reads as Christian-business religious-liberty defense — Armed Forces Brewing is military-themed not religious, so the vote does not map and the cell stays null per strict cite-only discipline (the Dec 2023 cite is preserved in the cross-cutting biblical_marriage q4 note above as the LGBTQ-as-Norfolk-value evidence). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Sheriff Joe Baron is listed as a personal endorser on her endorsements page but a single sheriff endorsement ≠ FOP endorsement; campaign Safe Neighborhoods page lists 'increased pay for Norfolk's public-safety team members ... to resolve recruitment and retention challenges' but this is routine governance budget language not affirmative pro-cop advocacy (the FXBG high-bar rule). NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Norfolk has no 287(g); no individually-attributed Doyle vote on sanctuary status located; ACLU-VA has pressured the Sheriff but the council-level posture remains undefined → null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / christian_liberty q2-q4 / election_integrity / refuse_overreach q0,q1,q3,q4 / family_child_sovereignty / biblical_marriage q0 q2 — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 20% — coherent center-left urban Democratic council member with one fiscal-restraint TRUE on the FY25 rate cut. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, any 2026 forum positions, and a focused dig into Norfolk meeting minutes for individual sanctuary / library-content votes.

Phil Hernandez, Mayor Kenneth Alexander (all D), former Del. Jay Jones, former Del. Paula Miller (D), and EAN-PACE (teacher union); Progressive Voters Guide named him the more progressive choice in his race (with reservations about his tax-cut stance). Notably no GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. DEVIATION FLAG: significant Christian-identity and fiscal-restraint signals counter the D-coalition pattern — 'home preacher' designation + Navy veteran + explicit support for the recent property-tax-rate reduction + YIMBY/Realtors/Chamber business backing = honest counter-archetype softener. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: campaign Housing page (https://www.mcgeefornorfolk.com/housing) states 'I supported the recent property tax rate reduction and believe we should continue to carefully consider the impact of property taxes on housing affordability' (the May 14 2024 FY25 2¢ rate cut PREDATES his Jan 1 2025 swearing-in, so he didn't cast that vote himself), AND as a sitting member he voted YES on the FY26 Norfolk budget (May 12 2026) that held the rate at $1.23 with no increase (per Norfolk meeting docket). Two-vector fiscal-restraint TRUE: stated pro-cut preference + actual hold-the-line vote. Progressive Voters Guide explicitly flagged 'concern over McGee's support for reducing taxes that could help fund needed services,' corroborating his fiscal-restraint posture from the LEFT. SPARSE-DATA CAVEAT: ALL OTHER 29 LOCAL cells NULL per strict cite-only discipline — he took office only ~17 months ago, keeps a deliberately narrow policy lane (housing, basic city services, business-friendliness, fiscal restraint), and has stayed publicly silent on culture-war / Christian-conservative rubric items. NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR): his stated 'funding, more staffing, and the right work environments for our first responders' lumps police with fire/EMS, no FOP endorsement located (only IAFF Local 68 firefighters + Sheriff Joe Baron personal endorsement, not the same as FOP); routine 'fund first responders' does NOT clear affirmative-advocacy bar. NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: Norfolk has annual Pride proclamations but no individually-attributed McGee vote or dissent located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located. 1 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells (=100% on cited but the denominator is essentially 1, which is the textbook sparse-data trap — the 100% reflects research diligence on a narrowly-focused first-term councilmember, NOT broad rubric alignment). Recommend revisiting after his first full FY27 budget cycle and any 2027/28 culture-war votes that may surface.
