candidates.name: Carlos J. Clanton
candidates.notes:
Superward 7 representative.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk City Council, Superward 7 (citywide), sworn 1/1/2025; term to 12/31/2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Prior service: Norfolk School Board Ward 3 since 7/1/2018, served as Vice Chair and Chair before resigning for council — that school-board record is where most of his scoreable policy footprint lives. HARD adverse evidence on biblical_marriage q2/q4 + family_child_sovereignty q2: as a school-board member, on May 2023 Clanton voted YES on Norfolk Public Schools' adoption of 'Get Real,' a Planned-Parenthood-developed comprehensive sex-ed curriculum for grades 6-10. Per WAVY/WTKR/13NewsNow coverage of the meeting, 'Get Real' content includes 'gender and sexual identity,' homosexuality, lesbianism, masturbation, abortion, condoms; the board passed it 6-1 (only Rodney Jordan dissented). Clanton was quoted on the record: 'I will be voting in support of this.' HARD adverse evidence on family_child_sovereignty q1: on the Youngkin 2023 transgender model policies (parental notification, biological-sex facilities) Clanton said: 'I support each student. I support the value of parental involvement, but at the end of the day as a member of the school board and education, I have to put children first.' That formulation explicitly subordinates parental rights to board judgment — directly adverse to the rubric's parental-notification/consent question. HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: campaign-priorities page explicitly says 'Fully fund the Norfolk Police Department to ensure adequate resources'; he was also endorsed by Norfolk Sheriff Joe Baron — affirmative campaign-platform advocacy + sheriff endorsement clears the high bar. HARD adverse self_defense evidence: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' designation in 2018, 2022, AND 2024 — MDA designates only candidates who have demonstrated they 'will support stronger gun laws' (per skill: an endorsement by a single-issue group is HARD evidence on that issue's cells). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion stance separate from the curriculum vote), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement separate from the curriculum), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice position; he is a public-school-funding voice but has not explicitly opposed choice — left null), christian_liberty (any cell — Baptist choir membership is identity, not a policy stance), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — 'lower property tax rates by increasing revenue through economic development' is conventional municipal language), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach (the 'children first' framing on Youngkin policy cuts AGAINST the rubric's conservative-local-control intent and is already used on family_child_sovereignty q1; the cell about local control against unconstitutional state mandates is generally about resisting LIBERAL state mandates and Clanton has not done that — left null), border_immigration (any cell). Counter-archetype context: faith-active Baptist + Sheriff Baron endorsement + 'Fully fund NPD' — yet voted YES on the Planned-Parenthood-authored sex-ed curriculum and triple-MDA Gun Sense designee. The school-board record dominates the cultural cells.

candidates.name: Ramin Fatehi
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney (elected, partisan D). Second term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD adverse sanctity_of_life q0+q1 evidence: Fatehi is a confirmed co-signatory of the Sept 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution joint statement of 85 elected prosecutors refusing to prosecute abortion-related conduct. Direct quote on 13NewsNow: 'As the Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Norfolk, our chief minister of justice, I will never prosecute a person who seeks or has an abortion that would have been legal under Roe… what is right has not changed.' That is categorical declination of any future VA abortion restrictions — clears the rubric bar for FALSE on both the public-profession cell and the affirmative-CA-action cell. HARD adverse biblical_marriage q2 evidence: his office publicly committed to mandatory 'LGBTQ+ sensitivity' training for all prosecutors and explicitly markets itself as 'a welcome place for… members of the LGBTQ+ community' (fatehinorfolk.com/priorities) — adverse on the biological-sex/trans-policy cell. HARD adverse public_justice q1 evidence: as deputy CA under Greg Underwood he was primary author of Norfolk's 2019 policy 'rejecting cash bail as a tool for prosecution, instructing all Norfolk prosecutors to cease demanding cash bail'; his stated legislative priority is to 'lobby to end all cash bail in Virginia,' quoted 'Nobody should have to pay their way out of jail.' Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy — adverse to the rubric's pretrial-detention cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: per The Appeal post-election coverage, Fatehi 'says crime is a symptom of structural racism' and his campaign site centers root-causes/public-health framing — literal embrace of the public-health-of-violence framing the rubric scores against. HARD adverse border_immigration q1 evidence: Fatehi co-founded the 2025-26 'FAFO' (Fight Against Federal Overreach) coalition with Larry Krasner and others, publicly vowing to prosecute federal ICE/CBP agents who violate VA law — quote: 'Just because you have a gun and a badge issued by a particular agency does not make you immune to prosecution for crimes under the Virginia code.' Active anti-ICE-cooperation posture; Norfolk on CIS sanctuary-jurisdictions map. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: in 13NewsNow/NRA-ILA/Bearing Arms coverage of the March 2026 Old Dominion University terror attack, Fatehi blamed gun-rights advocacy rather than the foreign-born felon perpetrator: 'Americans care more about guns than children… until there is the political will to break the spell of the cult of gun absolutism, you will see more incidents like this.' When given a Fox News opportunity to walk back, he said: 'I absolutely stand by what I said. It is the truth, no matter how much the gun lobby wants to deny it.' profile.background: Born Suffolk, VA. Brief DC corporate practice post-clerkship, then Richmond public defender, then Norfolk Office of the Commonwealth's Attorney from ~2006 (left briefly, returned 2012). Deputy Commonwealth's Attorney under Greg Underwood — authored Norfolk's 2019 cash-bail-rejection policy and the marijuana-possession declination policy. Elected Norfolk Commonwealth's Attorney Nov 2021 (took office 1/1/2022); re-elected unopposed in Nov 4 2025 general after defeating challenger John Butler (~5 pts) in the June 24 2025 D primary — second term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Soros-network funded: ~$650K total across 2021-2025 cycles from Justice & Public Safety PAC, Democracy PAC, and Real Justice PAC (Fox/NRA-ILA/VPAP). Endorsed by Real Justice PAC, NIAC Action, IAPAC. Member, Fair and Just Prosecution coalition. Co-founded 2025-26 'FAFO' (Fight Against Federal Overreach) coalition with Larry Krasner et al. Wife Mary Beth (Master Lecturer at Old Dominion University); sons Thomas and James (Washington Blade 3/4/2021 profile cites his 'LGBTQ sons' as shaping policy).

candidates.name: Robert M. 'Bobby' Dyer
candidates.notes: [2026-06-04 18:30 RESOLUTE refinement] Dense local record after 7+ years as Mayor + 14 yrs as Centerville Councilman. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 7T / 4F / 19 NULL = 64% (11 scored cells). HARD TRUE christian_liberty q0 — Regent alumni interview faith-profession in office: 'I honestly felt all along that the good Lord had his hand on my shoulder'; 'you can't do it by yourself … so I pray'; 'I really think that the big answer to a lot of social ills is going to be grounded in faith.' SOFT TRUE christian_liberty q3 — keynote at Regent Veterans Prayer Breakfast + post-2019 mass shooting call for prayer / framing event as 'an act of evil.' HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — explicit 'I will never ever tell a police stand down' op-ed defense of police funding ('Defunding Beach police would imperil city'); FOP+PBA endorsements; created Mayor's Task Force for a Safer Virginia Beach. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 — repeatedly pressed Northam to relax COVID restrictions (July 23 2020 letter; Labor Day 2020 restaurant-restriction rollback request; pushed for Phase-1 beach opening). HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q2 — Aug 12 2021 Council meeting on the record: 'masks really don't give much protection' citing (later-retracted) studies; stood by comment after media criticism. SOFT TRUE border_immigration q1+q2 — May 2025 DHS sanctuary list pushback: city office explicitly rejected sanctuary status + Dyer campaign quote 'in Virginia Beach, where law and order matters, if someone is found to be illegally in the country, ICE can do what they're going to do'; VB has no sanctuary ordinance under his tenure. HARD TRUE self_defense q1 — Dyer CALLED the Jan 6 2020 special Council session and VOTED YES on the 6-4 Virginia Beach Second Amendment Constitutional City resolution (vs Henley/Jones/Rouse/Wooten NO) — explicitly pushing back on Richmond's then-pending red-flag/AWB/registry package, 7 months after the May 2019 Municipal Center mass shooting at his own City Hall complex; insisted on 'Constitutional City' NOT 'Sanctuary' language ('precedent and a slippery slope') — heterodox framing but still HARD YES on the affirmative 2A vote. HARD FALSES on biblical_marriage: q4 — Mayor's office issued June 2020 official LGBT Pride Month proclamation for VB; q2 — Oct 2019 publicly ousted Human Rights Commission member LaKendrick Coburn El over anti-gay/anti-trans Facebook posts ('no longer appropriate'); q0 SOFT FALSE — VB Mayor's office formally supports state-level LGBTQ nondiscrimination ('should not be legal to deny someone the opportunity to work, rent a home, or be served in public accommodation based on sexual orientation or gender identity'); all-gender single-stall restrooms in muni facilities. Heterodox catches noted in meta. Recommend revisit after any 2026 Pride proclamation update / school-board liaison action.

claims.text: Mayor Dyer has consistently kept Virginia Beach's property tax rate the lowest in the Hampton Roads region throughout his tenure, publicly committing to 'a careful balance of high citizen services with the lowest property taxes in the region.' During the FY2025-26 budget cycle the City Council held public hearings on City Manager Patrick Duhaney's proposed $2.8 billion operating budget without raising the real property tax rate — a fiscal discipline posture consistent with the rubric's anti-deficit/balanced-budget standard.

In 2026, Mayor Dyer assembled the Mayor's Task Force for a Safer Virginia Beach — a cross-sector advisory panel of community leaders and stakeholders charged with developing public safety recommendations for City Council. The Task Force is expected to submit its findings in late 2026. Dyer is also the first U.S. Marine Corps veteran ever directly elected as mayor of Virginia Beach (served 1968-72), bringing a military discipline and public-order perspective to city leadership.

Nothing's getting cheaper.' Affirmative sausage-making defense of the regressive bundle Henley alone opposed. NULL everywhere else. HETERODOX CATCHES: (1) Labor-coalition endorsement stack VBEA + IAFF + Teamsters + PBA — public-sector-union backed campaign; voted YES on the April 30, 2024 5-5-1 collective-bargaining resolution (failed on Ross-Hammond abstention) alongside Wooten/Rouse/Schulman/Remick, defending the ~$1M annual cost as offset by retention savings; (2) RPVB lists him without the '(R)' label other Republicans carry — claims the seat but the official does not campaign openly as R. BACKGROUND: ~40-yr Colliers International commercial real estate broker (Sr VP, industrial focus), former VA State Senate legislative aide 1984, Cox HS head tennis coach 2000-06. Quoted: 'we should never have a curfew' — disavowed extraordinary-policing tools in favor of more officers + substation buildout. Vote-against-restraint posture even with stated reservations — null'ing this on the grounds that he OPPOSED a piece would be too generous; the rubric prizes vote-bearing fiscal discipline. Parallel to the Norfolk-batch FALSEs on tax-rate-hold votes. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located in available sources despite extensive social-media + meeting-minutes review. The Episcopal Eastern Shore Chapel 'Inclusive Church' branding is congregational not personal; biblical_marriage q4 stays null absent a Remick-signed Pride proclamation or trans-policy vote. He was NOT on council for the Jan 2020 VB 2A Sanctuary vote (predates him by 3 years). 1 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells = 50% — coherent low-profile center-right/center-left independent Council member whose only firm public signals are affirmative pro-LE plus-up and FY26 tax-hike budget vote. Endorsed by AG Miyares + Youngkin (Crime Commission appointment) + Lt. Gov. Sears + Sheriff Holcomb. SOFT FALSE public_justice q3 — explicitly embraces public-health framing for SUBSTANCE ABUSE ('the link between mental health, substance abuse and criminal behavior is one of the most important issues we face'); runs Drug Treatment Court, Behavioral Health Docket, First Step (voluntary diversion to medical detox), Narcan distribution via asset-forfeiture funds. Pairs it with tough-on-violent-crime posture but rubric-flag framing IS present. NULL EVERYWHERE ELSE — striking total silence on pro-life, marriage, parental rights, 2A, ICE cooperation, sanctuary across 30 years of public service. Sibling political ecosystem: brother Ken (now-retired Sheriff who cooperated heavily with ICE — 78 inmate transfers, May 2025 DHS list pushback) is family network but Colin himself has NOT made the ICE statements his brother did (per discipline: do NOT score by family inference). CRITICAL CORRECTION TO PRIOR-SESSION NOTES: father is Ed Stolle (Navy 22yrs), NOT a state senator named 'Ken Sr.' — that confusion swaps father and older brother. Heterodox catches: (a) zero faith profession despite Catholic-school upbringing — atypical for R prosecutor in red coastal city, stays purely secular; (b) public-health framing on substance abuse is explicit q3 FALSE — counterweight to tough-on-violent-crime branding; (c) self-investigated own former prosecutor for embezzlement — strong integrity signal; (d) prosecuted Rep. Scott Taylor's 2018 petition fraud — willingness to take on intra-party Republican target.

claims.text: During the 2025 Virginia Beach Commonwealth's Attorney re-election campaign, Stolle publicly stated 'I am a strong supporter of the Second Amendment, own firearms and hunt,' affirming a personal pro-2A commitment consistent with the rubric's constitutional-carry ideal. While he distinguishes prosecutorial enforcement from constitutional adjudication (courts decide constitutionality, not his office), his stated personal and cultural alignment with lawful firearm ownership is on the record.

Stolle has made victim-rights reform a central platform issue: he argues that allowing a defendant convicted of a violent crime to unilaterally choose judge vs. jury sentencing — without any input from the victim — is an unjust process that denies victims a voice. Under his leadership the Virginia Beach Commonwealth's Attorney's Office grew from 17 to 50 attorneys and Virginia Beach attained some of the lowest crime rates in its history, earning Stolle the 2021 Robert F. Horan Award from the Virginia Association of Commonwealth's Attorneys.

candidates.name: Jimmy Gray
candidates.notes:
Took office Jan 1, 2025. Former Vice Mayor. Former firefighter. Succeeded Donnie Tuck who did not seek re-election. Term expires Dec 2028.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Hampton; elected Nov 5, 2024 (~70% over Richelle Wallace); sworn in Jan 8, 2025; current term ends Dec 31, 2028; next election 2028. Hampton officially nonpartisan; filed as Independent (VPAP committee 'Gray for Hampton City Mayor – Jimmy'). HARD evidence: (1) On MAY 30, 2025 issued a FORMAL LETTER on Hampton city letterhead rejecting the U.S. Department of Homeland Security's placement of Hampton on the federal 'sanctuary jurisdictions' list. Hampton.gov posted the scanned PDF (CreationDate Fri May 30 16:23:13 2025 EDT, Ricoh IM C4510 scanner). Gray on the record (WTKR): 'Our city council has not adopted any ordinances, policies or resolutions, classifying ourselves as a sanctuary city... our police department and the city are cooperating when [we] are requested to assist with any execution of warrants or other activities involving Homeland Security agents working in our city.' → border_immigration q2 TRUE (affirmative rejection of sanctuary policy + endorsed federal-preemption posture). (2) Joint press briefing with Hampton Police Chief Jimmie Wideman on the surge in violence — Mayor Gray publicly partnered with the Chief, told residents to 'lock up your guns around your house, so if you have a break in, they can't steal your weapons,' and praised state-police partnership on 'street interdiction and traffic enforcement' as 'very effective' at getting 'guns and drugs off the street.' Combined with FY26 budget priorities to fund competitive police salaries — affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met → public_justice q0 TRUE. (3) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Gray was in the YES majority and publicly defended pay raises for city employees; this is routine governance with growing topline spending (5.4%), not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense → null on economic_stewardship q2 per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. (4) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance (5-2 YES, with Brown/Ferebee NO) — Gray voted YES and framed it as continuing to be 'the compassionate community we strive to be'; he said the ordinance 'would be applied evenly to anyone in violation' — public-order ordinance, NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy by itself. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) Gray serves on the boards of Providence Bible College & Theological Seminary AND Bernadine Franciscan Sisters Foundation — religious-board service is real but does not by itself satisfy christian_liberty q0 (high-bar public-square Christian profession requires an active public profession like Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment court argument; Gray has not made one in available sources) → christian_liberty cells null. (b) Hampton city government earned a 100/100 HRC Municipal Equality Index score and sponsors the Phabulous Phoebus Pride Festival — Gray has NOT personally signed a Pride proclamation that is documented in available sources; the sponsorship is institutional/CVB-led, so biblical_marriage and christian_liberty q4 are null pending a direct personal-signature finding. (c) On Feb 27, 2026 Gray accepted the Virginia Center for Inclusive Communities (Peninsula) Humanitarian Award and framed his work as 'building bridges, creating space at the table, and ensuring that no child, and no adult, feels unseen or unheard' — diversity/inclusion language but specifically racial/ethnic/religious framing, not LGBTQ-specific → null. (d) State of the City Dec 3, 2025 'Level Up' speech praised Hampton City Schools' graduation rates; not a curriculum/parental-rights statement → null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (no marriage/sexuality policy statement and no personally signed Pride proclamation located), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (board service to religious institutions is biographical, not active public-square advocacy), economic_stewardship q2 (routine budget YES with 5.4% growth not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform/restitution/public-health-framing/sword-of-state statement; Gray's gun-violence framing is shared-responsibility, not specifically magistrate-sword), refuse_state_overreach (chairs VML Legislative Committee but no specific Dillon-Rule pushback documented), border_immigration q1 (no statement on deportation of minors), self_defense (gun-violence framing emphasizes secure storage but no 2A-rights affirmation and no anti-2A position either).

profile.background: James 'Jimmy' Gray Jr.; age ~69 at time of 2024 election. Retired public-safety executive: former Hampton Fire Chief (6 years) and former Hampton Assistant City Manager for Public Safety & Human Services (retired 2014); 26 prior years with Chesapeake Fire Department. Four children, three grandchildren. Affiliations: NAACP Hampton Branch, Sigma Pi Phi (the Boulé). Civic: chairs the Virginia Municipal League Legislative Committee; long Hampton-Newport News regional service. Prior Hampton City Council member 2016, 2018, 2022 terms; Vice Mayor 2018-2024. Elected Mayor of Hampton Nov 5, 2024 over retired Hampton fire captain Richelle Wallace ~70%-29%. Sworn in Jan 8, 2025 at Hampton Roads Convention Center. Current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028. Hampton elections are officially nonpartisan; he filed and ran as Independent (VPAP). Not up again until 2028. Distinguished from any other 'Jimmy/James Gray.'

candidates.name: Steven L. Brown
candidates.notes:
Vice Mayor. Hampton council is at-large (no wards).

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor (selected by council 4-3 on Jan 8, 2025) and At-Large Hampton City Council member; second council term Jan 1, 2023 – Dec 31, 2026 (re-elected Nov 8, 2022; first elected July 1, 2018). Hampton officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. On the Nov 2026 ballot for re-election. RELIGIOUS PROFILE: B.A. Biblical Studies + M.A. Ministry + honorary doctorate + church associations (Calvary Community, Victory Life) — formal seminary/ministry credentials, but no public profession of Christ from the dais, no invocation/Sabbath/marriage stance, and no biblical-worldview policy statement located in available sources — notable absence for someone with formal credentials. HARD evidence: (1) APRIL 8, 2026 Hampton camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 — Brown was one of the two NAMED NO votes (joined by CM Michelle Ferebee). No direct on-record Brown quote located explaining the NO; Ferebee's adjacent quote ('not the right solution') is the only contemporaneous rationale on the record — public-order ordinance, not framed as anti-police, so public_justice q0 stays null. (2) WAVY 2022 candidate profile, on his own record, framed youth gun violence as 'a national health crisis' and prescribed conflict-resolution training: 'I strongly believe we reduce gun violence when we teach our youth and adults to learn how to resolve conflict versus reacting by picking up a gun and settling your differences.' → public_justice q3 FALSE (explicit framing of violent crime — youth gun violence — as a 'national health crisis' is exactly the public-health framing the rubric q3 singles out as the contrary position). (3) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 — Brown was in the 6-vote YES majority (Campbell lone NO); routine governance, not fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: Facebook page tagline (indexed snippet) reads 'leadership for a more progressive Hampton' — soft left self-identification, but the literal phrase alone does not satisfy any specific rubric cell. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) The seminary/ministry credentials with NO documented public profession is a notable absence; the rubric's high bar (FXBG/Bell precedent) requires active public-square profession, which Brown has not delivered in available sources. (b) Public-health framing on gun violence is the single strongest single-cell signal in his record. (c) The April 2026 camping NO joined the local NAACP-aligned dissent against criminalization of homelessness — not a defunding-police statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride; no Brown individual vote/statement), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; campaigned 2018 on fully funding K-12 — generic), christian_liberty (formal ministry credentials are biographical/educational, not active public-square Christian-symbols/profession statement per the high bar), economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity, public_justice q0/q1/q2/q4 (no pro-LE affirmative statement; no bail/restitution/sword-of-state position), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Brown made no individual statement), self_defense (gun-violence framing is public-health/conflict-resolution; no specific constitutional-carry or anti-red-flag/anti-AWB statement located).

candidates.name: Hope L. Harper
candidates.notes:
At-large council member.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, second term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2028; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 leading 9 candidates with 18.62%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; she ran as Independent on the ballot. THIN ISSUE FOOTPRINT — campaign site (hope4hampton.org) has no policy/Issues page; WAVY-stated priorities are 'more opportunities for youth and families,' 'lowering gun violence,' and 'adding more employment' — generic. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: member of Bethel Temple Church, an Assemblies of God Pentecostal congregation; church affiliation is on her official Hampton.gov bio. INSTITUTIONAL ROLES: serves on Hampton Planning Commission AND the Citizens Police Advisory Group AND Hampton Redevelopment & Housing Authority — an institutional-insider profile. DOCUMENTED VOTES: (a) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 ($677.9M, 1¢ real-estate tax-rate cut from $1.15 → $1.14/$100, 6-1 with Campbell the lone NO) — Harper was in the 6-vote YES majority by elimination; routine governance vote that includes a tax cut already engineered by staff is NOT scored as fiscal-restraint advocacy per the rubric high-bar (consistent with the Wolfenbarger precedent). (b) April 2026 camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 (Class 2 misdemeanor); Vice Mayor Brown and CM Ferebee were the named NOs — Harper was in the YES majority by elimination, but this is a public-order ordinance, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the rubric q0 bar. (c) Hampton University polling-site approval (unanimous council) — ballot-access expansion, not an integrity measure. ON GUN VIOLENCE (per WAVY 2024 candidate profile): she framed it as 'a national issue that has impacted [our] communities greatly' and called for 'a community effort' and 'proven methods' — vague community-effort framing, not affirmative pro-LE advocacy and not enough to flag the rubric's public-health framing under q3. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride and has an LGBTQ+ liaison, but Harper has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (Bethel Temple AG membership is identity, not a public-square Christian-symbols/prayer/conscience-rights statement), economic_stewardship (routine YES on a 6-1 budget with a rate cut already in the package is not fiscal-restraint advocacy in the rubric q2 sense — distinguish from Campbell's lone-NO dissent demanding more cuts), election_integrity, public_justice (institutional CPAG service and inferred YES on camping ordinance are not affirmative pro-LE advocacy per the high bar), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter, but Harper made no individual statement), self_defense.

candidates.name: Michelle Ferebee
candidates.notes:
At-large council member.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, current term Jan 1, 2025 – Dec 31, 2026 (special-election remainder of Jimmy Gray's seat after Gray moved up to Mayor); won Nov 5, 2024 special election with 57.67%. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; ballot label Independent. Will be on the ballot Nov 2026 for re-election per her campaign site. RELIGIOUS IDENTITY: explicitly discloses 'Active member of St. Vincent de Paul Catholic Church' on her campaign About page — unusually direct religious disclosure for a nonpartisan Hampton candidate. HARD evidence: (1) APRIL 8, 2026 Hampton camping/sleeping-on-public-property ordinance passed 5-2 — Ferebee was one of the two NAMED NO votes (joined by Vice Mayor Steven Brown); she explained her NO on the record: 'After months of work, it represents a solution. But from our perspective, it's not the right solution.' Hampton NAACP opposed the ordinance. The ordinance is a Class 2 misdemeanor (up to 6 months / $1,000 fine) — public_justice q0 does NOT score her FALSE on this alone because the q0 bar is opposition to defunding/dismantling police, not opposition to a specific public-order ordinance; her NO appears framed in compassion/homelessness terms, not anti-LE terms. → public_justice q0 null. (2) FY26 Hampton budget May 2025 — Ferebee was in the 6-vote YES majority (Campbell lone NO); also asked specifically about grant-funded programs in the March 12, 2025 budget retreat — routine governance, NOT fiscal-restraint advocacy → economic_stewardship q2 null per the Wolfenbarger high-bar precedent. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: Issues page lists ONLY Livability / Technology & Innovation / Coastal Resilience — deliberately apolitical good-government profile with NO culture-war positioning in either direction. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but Ferebee has no individual vote/statement on a proclamation, drag, or trans policy), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT/SOGI statement; Hampton schools are a separate elected body), christian_liberty (open Catholic identity on campaign site is religious identification but not a public-square Christian-symbols/conscience-rights statement per the high bar — distinguish from Anton Bell's Sixth Commandment invocation in open court which earned christian_liberty q0 TRUE), economic_stewardship q2 (routine YES on a 6-1 budget is not fiscal-restraint advocacy), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (camping NO is not in itself the contrary of any q0–q4 cell), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton rejected DHS sanctuary designation in May 2025 via Mayor Gray's letter; Ferebee made no individual statement), self_defense.

candidates.name: Carolyn Campbell
candidates.notes:
1st Term: Jan 1, 2025 - Dec 31, 2028. Newly elected.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, elected Nov 5, 2024 (~14.81%, 3rd of 9 for 3 seats), sworn in Jan 2025, term ends Dec 31, 2028. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; Campbell is publicly listed on the Hampton GOP 'Elected Officials' page (party-in-practice Republican-aligned). HARD evidence: (1) LONE NO vote on the May 2025 FY26 Hampton budget — she dissented because the 1-cent real-estate-tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100 didn't go far enough; quote: 'we just have to balance that out with what the people can stomach'; campaign platform explicitly opposes 'unnecessary taxes and fees' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (fiscal-restraint posture documented by a roll-call dissent in the tax-cutting direction); (2) explicit campaign-platform statement: 'Hampton must support the parents' right to voice their concerns, allow an open forum for suggestions, and ensure parents that their children are safe...' Daughter of a Hampton police officer; platform states 'Carolyn supports the integrity of the police officers to keep law and order' and 'Policing in our community needs to be supported with full staffing and resources'; voted YES on April 2026 Hampton ordinance banning camping/sleeping on public property (5-2 majority), saying 'I know this ordinance is not perfect, but it's a start' — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met with explicit platform + family LE tie + roll-call vote on a public-order ordinance). NULL on family_child_sovereignty q1 — her 'parents' right to voice their concerns' is general parental-voice language, NOT the specific parental notification/consent on medical/mental-health/gender interventions the rubric q1 requires (per the FXBG high-bar discipline: vague slogans → null). NULL on self_defense q0/q1 — Lafayette Gun Club membership + 'law-abiding citizens' rights to self-defense' is supportive 2A rhetoric but not specifically constitutional-carry-without-permit and not specifically opposition to red-flag/mag-limit/AWB/registries (no Moms Demand / Gun Sense endorsement either; that would have been a hard FALSE — she has neither). NULL on election_integrity q1/q4 — Election Integrity Task Force membership and Officer of Election service are activist signals but do not map to specific photo-ID-with-citizenship-verification or anti-Zuckerbucks rubric cells without a direct citable statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton has an LGBTQ+ liaison page and sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but no Campbell-personal vote/statement on a Pride proclamation or marriage), christian_liberty (active Lutheran church involvement is identity, not a specific public-square Christian-symbols / prayer / Sabbath / conscience-rights statement per the high-bar rule), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform / restitution / public-health-framing / sword-of-state statement), refuse_state_overreach (no Dillon-Rule pushback or state-mandate-resistance vote during tenure located), border_immigration (Hampton formally rejected sanctuary label and cooperates with ICE, but that was Mayor Gray's public statement, not Campbell's). Distinguished from Karolyn Campbell (NC, unrelated) and a separate 'Carolyn C. Campbell' candidate elsewhere.

profile.background: Anton A. Bell; first African-American elected Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney. candidates.name: Phillip D. Jones
candidates.notes:
27th Mayor. City's youngest directly elected mayor. Ran as independent but strong Democratic ties (endorsed by Gov. McAuliffe, Sen. Locke; member of Majority Democrats). Term expires Dec 2026.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Newport News (officially nonpartisan office; the Mayor IS a voting member of council per Newport News Charter §3.04, 'a voting member of the council'); D-leaning per inaugural 'Majority Democrats' membership and McAuliffe/Locke endorsements. HARD adverse 2A evidence: on 11/26/2024 the 6-1 ordinance EXPANDING the city firearms ban (deleting 'openly' so concealed-carry permit holders also barred from carrying in city buildings/parks; only Eley dissented) — Jones was in the 6-vote majority per the charter's voting-mayor rule AND CHAMPIONED the ordinance on the record: 'The safety and security of citizens, of employees, and residents, is top of mind'; 'This is not an infringement of anyone's right. This is … taking steps to protect everyone.' Jones disclosed he himself holds a concealed-carry permit and supported the ordinance anyway. HARD adverse LGBT-promotion evidence: as Mayor, Jones officially proclaimed June as Pride Month in BOTH 2023 ('NN mayor proclaims June as Pride Month; City to host first Pride festival' — WTKR 6/2023) and 2025 ('I AM WHAT I AM: Newport News Pride Festival' — Mayor's proclamation 6/28/2025). These are Mayor's-office proclamations (not council votes), and they're affirmative state promotion of LGBTQ identity. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: publicly framed gun violence as a 'public health crisis' in an Everytown press release ('Gun violence is a public health crisis affecting our nation, and I commend the Everytown Community Safety Fund for seeing the benefits of investing in community health systems'). HARD positive public_justice q0 evidence: repeated affirmative pro-police advocacy beyond bare budget votes — 'We're really doubling down on public safety here in Newport News' (FY27 budget) and 'We have a budget that invests number one in our public safety' (FY25 budget) — clears the high bar. HARD adverse border q2 evidence: joint statement with NNPS School Board Chair Lisa Surles-Law on federal ICE actions in schools (Jan 2025) — NNPS schools 'must remain safe havens'; 'protection of all residents — regardless of immigration status'; 'We will not allow federal immigration policies to disrupt the educational experience or hinder the progress of our youth' — sanctuary-style posture against federal immigration enforcement. SEPARATE deviation context (not directly rubric-scored): Jones authored an op-ed (FairVote, 11/20/2024) endorsing expansion of ranked-choice voting to all VA local elections including mayoral races — cuts against the spirit of election_integrity simplicity, but rubric cells q0/q1/q4 are about paper ballots/voter ID/Zuckerbucks specifically, none of which Jones has addressed; left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no pro-life or pro-choice proclamation), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (no school-choice/parental-consent/CRT-SOGI position), christian_liberty (no profession of Christ — Hampton Christian Academy is HS-only, not a personal-faith statement), economic_stewardship q2 (no BBA/deficit framing — the FY budgets are routine governance), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0-q4, border_immigration q1, self_defense q0 separately from q1 (the firearms vote/championing affirms BOTH cells against constitutional carry posture). Religion: NO current church affiliation cited; Hampton Christian Academy HS attendance is the only data point and is not a profession of faith. Counter-archetype flag: CCW-permit-holding USMC officer who sponsors the city firearms-buildings ban — unusual.

profile.background: Born 9/19/1989 at Kadena AFB, Okinawa (USAF parents — father F-15 pilot, mother KC-135 navigator). USMC infantry officer 1st Bn/8th Marines 2012-2018 active; currently Major, USMCR. Bain & Company social-impact/public-sector consultant. Newport News Planning Commission ~2 yrs prior to mayoral run. Wife: Jamila Wynter, M.D. Omega Psi Phi. Inaugural member, 'Majority Democrats' coalition (Democratic Mayors Assn); Wikipedia/press categorize him under VA Democrats despite the 2022 independent ballot label. Endorsed in 2022 by Gov. Terry McAuliffe and Sen. Mamie Locke. 27th Mayor of Newport News and the city's youngest directly-elected mayor; term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on Nov 2026 ballot — filing deadline 6/16/2026; per WAVY, campaign said he 'will announce his reelection campaign this fall'; convicted former Del. Phil Hamilton (R) exploring a challenge).

candidates.name: Tina L. Vick
candidates.notes:
South District representative. Sworn in for 2025-2028 term.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, South District 3 / Seat A, in her FIFTH four-year term (since May 2008) — former Vice Mayor 2016-2020; lost the 2022 mayoral race to Jones (3rd, 18.8%). (2) She also voted YES on the original Oct 2020 open-carry ban (6-1, with former Councilwoman Patricia Woodbury the sole NO). Two distinct roll calls on local gun-control expansions. Voted YES with 7-0 majority on the FY2026 operating budget (5/13/2025) and was at the FY2027 budget discussion 5/2026 — routine governance, scored null on economic_stewardship q2 (BBA framing). candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — STATUS UPDATE] Howard E. Gwynn lost the June 17, 2025 Democratic primary 57.15%-42.85% to Shannon M. Jones (former Asst. Commonwealth's Attorney). No Republican filed for November 2025; Jones was elected unopposed in the Nov 2025 general and took office Jan 1, 2026. Gwynn's term as Newport News Commonwealth's Attorney ENDED 12/31/2025 — as of this refinement (2026-06) he has been OUT OF OFFICE for ~5 months. Marked status=lost / candidacy_status=lost per the refine-scorecard skill (lost-primary rule). HARD evidence (preserved for the historical record): founding-era member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (VPPFJ), the alliance whose formal platform demands ending cash bail, abolishing the death penalty, ending mandatory minimums, ending the three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny, and expanding expungement (Charlottesville.gov letter to VA General Assembly, Mar 8, 2021). Progressive Voters Guide 2021 attributed to him support for 'ending three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny offenses, and ending cash bail' (public_justice q1 FALSE). Did not declare a marijuana-non-prosecution policy like Morales / Descano / Dehghani-Tafti. Highest-profile decision: declined to charge the 6-year-old Richneck Elementary shooter (Jan 2023; 'a 6-year-old cannot form the requisite criminal intent') and charged the assistant principal with felony child neglect. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality, family/schools, Christian liberty, immigration/ICE, self-defense / 2A (charging policies on those topics not located), refuse_state_overreach (no declared non-prosecution policy), church/faith affiliation. candidates.notes: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] 5+ years as Mayor + 10 years on Council = densest possible local record. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 4T / 3F / 23 NULL = 57.1% — coherent moderate-Independent pragmatist profile, not a Christian-conservative profile. HARD FALSES: (a) signed June 2021 official City of Suffolk Mayor's proclamation declaring 'LGBTQ Pride Month' for inaugural Suffolk Pride at Bennett's Creek Park → biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; (b) backed/oversaw Oct 2021 city employee COVID vaccine-or-testing mandate, drew formal threat-to-sue letter from his own City Prosecutor, did not reverse → refuse_state_overreach q2 FALSE; (c) participated in Mar 29 2022 Hampton Roads Regional Mayors Forum that explicitly framed gun violence as 'public health crisis,' supplied 'learn from successes/failures…be more efficient with resources' quote, did not push back on framing → public_justice q3 FALSE. HARD TRUE: voted YES Jan 15 2020 on Suffolk 'Constitutional City' 2A resolution which named & opposed the then-pending VA red-flag/magazine-limit/AWB/registry package (7-0; Mayor Linda T. Johnson absent that night) AND publicly stated Dec 2019 at SB16 hearing that proposed VA firearms restrictions would make many Suffolk residents (including himself) felons + he would 'not favor the city expending resources to enforce them' → self_defense q1 TRUE HARD. SOFT TRUES: same Dec 2019 statement + Jan 2020 vote map to refuse_state_overreach q0 (asserted local control vs state), q1 (declined state strings), q4 (refused to enforce unconstitutional state directives). SOFT FALSE: Apr-Jun 2026 Suffolk Council moved to BLOCK ICE office siting on Wilroy Road (City Manager Hughes drafted letters to large-industrial owners; Council revised industrial-zoning proffers to prohibit detention uses); Duman did not dissent or break from collective city posture → border_immigration q1 FALSE SOFT. NULL on remaining 23 cells per evidence-first discipline: no profession of Christ (civic prayer-breakfast hosting is ceremonial, not credo); ceremonial swearing-in praise of SPD does NOT meet HIGH BAR for public_justice q0; FY26 budget held rate at $1.07 producing ~4.71% effective tax increase via reassessment with sole NO Tim Johnson pushing 2¢ cut, FY27 unanimous 1¢ cut → economic_stewardship q2 mixed-signal NULL per discipline (held rate is not a hike but is not a cut either; Bennett & Johnson pushed harder for restraint). Suffolk NOT on May 30 2025 DHS sanctuary list (Portsmouth was); no formal sanctuary ordinance → border_immigration q2 NULL. Recommend revisiting after any 2026 Pride/sanctuary/ICE/2A/abortion action by Council.

profile.background: Mayor of City of Suffolk, Virginia (officially nonpartisan; Independent on filings; 2024 endorsed by Hampton Roads Black Caucus — center-Independent record, not R-aligned). Age 75. Elected Mayor Nov 3, 2020 (defeated incumbent Linda T. Johnson); re-elected Nov 5, 2024 in 3-way at-large race (34.9% Duman vs 34.5% Jenkins vs 30.3% Bosselman — 161-vote margin). Term Jan 2025 - Dec 2028; publicly announced he will NOT seek re-election 2028. Prior service: Suffolk City Council, Chuckatuck Borough, Jan 3, 2011 - Dec 2020 (~10 yrs; re-elected 2014/2018). Suffolk EDA, Western Tidewater Regional Jail Board, Western Tidewater Water Authority. ODU grad; commissioned officer U.S. Army Reserve. Owns Mike Duman Auto Sales / Rental & Collision in Suffolk (founded 1980 — youngest Ford-Mercury dealer in U.S. at 29). Suffolk's 2024 'First Citizen.' Faith: no public profession of Christ; hosts annual civic Leadership Prayer Breakfast (National Day of Prayer) as Mayor — ceremonial.

candidates.name: Matthew R. Hamel
candidates.notes:
Elected 2022. Originally Republican, switched to Democrat Nov 2024. Won re-election Nov 2025 (57% vs 43% over David Mick R).

[2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Chesapeake Commonwealth's Attorney, re-elected 11/4/2025 (57-43 over GOP challenger Mick) as a Democrat after resigning from the Chesapeake GOP 11/5/2024; current term through Jan 1, 2030. HARD evidence: (1) public statement to WAVY/Yahoo (Sept 17, 2025) explicitly framing violent crime in 'public health' terms — 'Breaking the cycle of violent crime requires treating the illness and not just the symptoms' — directly contradicts public_justice q3 → q3 FALSE. Mixed-signal cases that did NOT score: (a) public_justice q0 — created an Elder Abuse Task Force partnering with local + federal LE and explicitly emphasized 'multi-agency task forces,' but no documented FOP / police-union endorsement and a 2025 PAAF (Bill Barr / Tim Griffin's Protecting Americans Action Fund) five-figure ad buy classified him among 'radical progressive prosecutors' for refusing to denounce Jay Jones's 'two bullets to the head' texts about Speaker Gilbert — net null on q0; (b) public_justice q1 — PAAF 'radical progressive prosecutor' classification + GOP challenger Mick's documented allegations (Brady-vacated murder conviction; speedy-trial-dismissed malicious-wounding case; <5% conviction rate on juvenile-weapons-at-school cases) are real concerns, BUT counter-evidence is Hamel's explicit statement that 'plea agreements are generally NOT appropriate for domestic violence and DUI cases' (WAVY 9/9/2025) AND no documented Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC / Real Justice PAC / Fair and Just Prosecution-letter signature — net null on q1 (mixed; not the Soros-prosecutor archetype the rubric singles out); (c) public_justice q2 — diversion-for-NONVIOLENT offenders (Drug/Recovery Court, Behavioral Health Court, veterans court; 'second largest Recovery Court in Virginia') does not directly contradict victim restitution / proportional punishment of evildoers (it addresses different offenders) → null. NO EVIDENCE on sanctity_of_life (was NOT a CA in 2020 so could not have signed the 2020 abortion-non-prosecution letter; no signature on 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution post-Dobbs joint statement; no individual statement located), biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty (juvenile-case prosecution record is mixed but doesn't map to school-choice/parental-notification/CRT cells), christian_liberty (Catholic parishioner is identity, no policy stance on q0/q2/q3/q4 located), economic_stewardship (statement that the CA's office is 'inadequately funded' is pro-spending posture but does not address deficit/balanced-budget rubric), election_integrity, public_justice q4 (no death-penalty / mandatory-minimums / asset-forfeiture statement located), refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Chesapeake Sheriff's separately-elected ICE IGSA expansion is not Hamel's record and he did NOT publicly oppose it — silence is not a scoreable position here), self_defense.

candidates.name: Shannon E. Glover
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Portsmouth (officially nonpartisan, D-aligned). Second term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD positive economic_stewardship q2 evidence: at the Rivers Casino two-year mark Glover publicly framed casino-revenue distribution as enabling Portsmouth to cut the real-estate tax rate from $1.30 to $1.23 and 'freeze other rates for residents,' with ~$5M of ~$20M annual casino take steered to tax reduction and $15M to schools (WTKR). The deliberate tax-cut framing of a casino revenue windfall — and the council action that implemented it — is the cleanest local-rubric fiscal-restraint signal for a Hampton Roads D mayor and clears the bar for TRUE on the rate-restraint cell. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: as a councilman Glover voted NO on Portsmouth's Jan 14, 2020 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution — it passed 4-3 over his opposition (with Mayor Rowe, VM Lucas-Burke). He has not reversed that posture as mayor and went on record expressing concerns about Councilman Nathan Clark bringing an AR-15 into a council meeting. Categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: at the Hampton Roads mayors' forum on youth violence (13NewsNow), Glover explicitly described youth violence as 'a public health crisis'; his Project Safe Neighborhood / Portsmouth United model centers 'trauma-trained healthcare professionals,' behavioral-health stakeholders, and clergy alongside (not above) police — textbook public-health-of-violence paradigm the rubric scores against. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Portsmouth has not taken up an abortion-related resolution under Glover), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), q4 (Portsmouth held its first-ever LGBTQ Pride Festival in 2022 during Glover's tenure but NO documentary evidence of a Glover-signed proclamation, City Hall Pride flag-raising, or council vote on Pride located — no scoreable cell; left null per skill discipline), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — mayor doesn't sit on Portsmouth Public Schools board; no statement located on school choice, parental notification, CRT/SOGI/library content), christian_liberty (any cell — single-vector 'faith in God' boilerplate on city bio is not a Captain-voice affirmative profession of Christ in office; no church affiliation surfaced; no public-square symbol/prayer/invocation advocacy or pastor-conscience protection located), economic_stewardship q4 (no specific federal-style BBA framing; q2 already captures the rate-cut win), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no statements on voter ID, paper ballots, or Zuckerbucks; Portsmouth council does not legislate state election law), public_justice q0 (his public-safety rhetoric centers 'partnership' and the 'strong arm of the law to enforce and hold them accountable' but does not clear the high bar — no quoted rejection of 'defund,' and his Crime & Gun Violence Reduction Commission framing is the same public-health body that drives the q3 FALSE — so cannot earn affirmative TRUE on q0 either), q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution-first / Romans-13 framing located), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — took office Jan 2021 post-peak COVID; no anti-state-mandate / ESG posture located), border_immigration q1/q2 (Portsmouth on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list and city spokesperson said city was 'researching' the listing rather than disavowing; no direct Glover quote on ICE cooperation located either way — left null), self_defense q1 (his concern about an AR-15 in council chambers is single-vector and not a vote on a CCW-restriction ordinance — q0 already captures the 2A posture; left null to avoid double-counting). Counter-archetype context: D mayor who EARNS a TRUE on the rate-cut cell via Rivers Casino windfall, on-archetype otherwise (NO on 2A sanctuary, public-health framing of violence, presided over Confederate-monument removal consensus vote). The two FALSEs and one TRUE are all quote-bearing and pass the SKILL's per-cell evidence bar.

candidates.name: William E. Moody Jr.
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth Vice Mayor + City Council, at-large (officially nonpartisan, R-leaning Independent by issue profile). Continuously elected since 1998, 7 confirmed terms; current term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026; PER WAVY 5/27/2026 he reversed course and IS seeking an 8th term in Nov 2026 (overrides the 1530 dossier's stale assumption). candidacy_status set general_candidate. HARD TRUE self_defense q0 + q1 evidence: as a long-serving councilmember he was a YES vote on the 1/14/2020 4-3 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution declaring Portsmouth a 2A sanctuary, and publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to bring an AR-15 into the council meeting that night — direct quote: 'I do not believe that Council[man] Clark needs to apologize for exercising his right as a law enforcement officer to carry his weapon to a public meeting.' The 2020 resolution was explicitly framed as opposition to the then-Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly's pending gun-control package (red-flag/AWB/UBC/mag-limits) — hard adverse to those restrictions. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 evidence: 2022 platform pledge 'a safe city with lower taxes' (campaign signature line per WAVY/Ballotpedia); voted YES on Portsmouth's FY25 budget that used Rivers Casino revenue to MAINTAIN a real-estate tax-rate DECREASE from $1.30 to $1.23 — same explicit-tax-cut framing the SKILL credited Mayor Glover for in the 1530 dossier (q2 TRUE precedent for Portsmouth councilmembers who voted YES on that budget). HARD TRUE public_justice q0 evidence (multi-vector affirmative pro-police advocacy, clears the high bar): (1) on 7/14/2020 he personally emailed Police Chief Angela Greene specifically demanding that the law be enforced and charges filed against the Confederate-monument vandals — NAACP attempted to have him criminally charged for the email, but a magistrate declined to issue process; (2) 2022 platform: 'The city is short 80 to 100 police officers… we need to maintain a full complement of 260 sworn officers' and restore the Community Impact Officer program; (3) publicly defended Councilman Nathan Clark's right to carry an AR-15 in chambers as a law-enforcement officer; (4) 2016: called BLM tunnel-blockers 'thugs' (later apologized — captures rhetorical posture against criminal-conduct framing). Multi-vector and quote-bearing — clears the affirmative-advocacy bar. HARD FALSE public_justice q3 evidence: voted YES on Portsmouth's gun-buyback program while admitting on the record 'studies show gun buyback programs aren't effective at obtaining guns from those who will use them to harm others' but adding 'it can't hurt' — i.e. acquiesced to the public-health framing of gun violence he himself didn't believe was efficacious. Affirmative participation in soft public-health framing → adverse on q3. HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q0 evidence: the 1/14/2020 2A resolution was an explicit assertion of local control against an unconstitutional state mandate the Democratic-trifecta VA General Assembly was preparing — Moody's direct quote: 'I can appreciate liberals' knee jerk reactions but if they have a problem with the law they should use the system they now control to change it.' Local-control posture against state overreach. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (no abortion-stance statement; Portsmouth council does not vote on this), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no marriage-definition or biological-sex statement), q4 (no Portsmouth council Pride proclamation vote has ever occurred — the 2022 Pride Festival was a private nonprofit event; absence of a vote is not scoreable per the 1530 SKILL discipline so q4 left null rather than provisionally TRUE), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — Portsmouth schools are governed by an elected School Board; Moody has no direct curriculum-vote record), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (his 2016 FOIA-litigation win against his own council (Moody v. Portsmouth, Judge Hammond ruled the council violated FOIA when fining him $1,500 for disclosing closed-session monument discussions — quote: 'good news for anybody who supports open government and transparency') is a clean transparency/free-speech victory but the rubric's christian_liberty q4 cell is specifically about state-funded promotion of non-Christian religious displays, which the FOIA win does not address; no cell maps cleanly, so the FOIA narrative is captured in profile context and left unscored), election_integrity q0/q1/q4, public_justice q1/q2/q4 (no cash-bail / restitution / Romans-13 framing — his 'use the system' line is procedural-democracy framing, not Romans-13 magisterial-sword framing), refuse_state_overreach q1/q2/q3/q4 (no COVID-mandate-resistance / ESG / subsidiarity statement located beyond q0), border_immigration q1/q2 (no Moody statement on Portsmouth's May 2025 DHS sanctuary listing or Sheriff Moore's WSO/287(g) cancellation; his 2019 'Squad' tweet was racial-framing not immigration-policy and is not scoreable). Counter-archetype context (NOT scored, but on audit trail): 2016 BLM 'thugs' remark (apologized), 2019 'really tired of hearing the term people of color' / 'Squad' tweet, Dorris v. Glover/Moody pending wrongful-termination suit alleging tax-assessment interference at Elizabeth Manor Country Club — negative-evidence flags for any 'principled-statesman' framing but do not contradict his R-leaning Independent archetype. Fix party Unknown→I; set candidacy_status=general_candidate (running 8th term, May 27 2026 reversal). 5-TRUE/1-FALSE on cited cells (~83% on a 6-cell denominator) honestly reflects a cultural-conservative populist with a verbalized 2A/police/local-control record plus one go-along-to-get-along gun-buyback misstep — coherent, not a sparse-data 100/0.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of a specific denominational affiliation or church home. When announcing his 2019 retirement (later retracted) he publicly quoted Ecclesiastes 3 ('To everything there is a season') — cultural-Christian framing without an affirmative profession of Christ in office or a named congregation.

profile.background: Portsmouth native, one of nine children. U.S. Air Force veteran (Loadmaster). 28 years at Doughtie's Foods (Port Norfolk), retired as VP Sales; then 10 years at U.S. Food Service Virginia Beach Division. Licensed Virginia real estate agent. Married to Mary Catterton (49 years); three children. Portsmouth City Council at-large (officially nonpartisan), continuously elected since 1998 — re-elected 2002, 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022 (current 7th four-year term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026). Vice Mayor 2004-2006, 2008-2010, and 2025-present (appointed by council Jan 2025; replaced VM Lisa Lucas-Burke who ran against Mayor Glover in 2024). Self-describes as the longest consecutively-serving councilmember in Portsmouth history. **Candidacy update (overrides 1530 dossier's stale assumption that he is not seeking re-election):** per WAVY May 27 2026 Moody REVERSED course and confirmed he WILL seek an 8th term in Nov 2026 — at least his third retirement reversal (also retracted in 2022 to seek his 7th term). Quoted: 'we have too much going on, especially with the waterfront development, to leave.' candidacy_status set general_candidate. Cross-cutting record: cultural-conservative populist (pro-2A, pro-police, pro-history, pro-transparency) operating in an officially-nonpartisan office. Counter-flag context: 2016 BLM-tunnel-protester 'thugs' remark (later apologized); 2019 'really tired of hearing the term people of color' / 'Squad' tweet drew NAACP rebuke; named defendant in Patrick Dorris v. Glover/Moody wrongful-termination suit alleging tax-assessment interference at Elizabeth Manor Country Club (unproven, pending). These don't contradict his archetype but are documented in the audit trail.

FY27 budget (May 12 2026) held the rate at $1.24 with no increase (https://www.wtkr.com/news/in-the-community/portsmouth/portsmouth-proposes-360-7-million-budget-with-no-tax-rate-increase-but-school-funding-falls-short) — Hugel in the YES majority. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared on MULTIPLE vectors): (1) Fraternal Order of Police Gosport Lodge #20 directly endorsed Hugel 2022 (https://www.markhugel.com/endorsements) — FOP endorsements are hard evidence per skill; (2) campaign / city bio describes him as having 'participated in several Portsmouth Police Department's R.E.S.E.T. walks, visiting the neighborhoods where homicides have occurred' — direct hands-on police-partnership engagement; (3) campaign plank 'Maintain competitive pay to provide for recruiting and retaining Police, Fire, and EMT personnel' — affirmative LE retention advocacy; (4) IAFF Local 539 firefighters endorsement reinforces public-safety-coalition support. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located. candidates.name: Stephanie N. Morales
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Portsmouth Commonwealth's Attorney (elected, partisan D). Third full term 1/1/2026-12/31/2029 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. HARD adverse sanctity_of_life q0+q1 evidence: Morales is a confirmed signatory of the June 24, 2022 Fair and Just Prosecution Joint Statement from Elected Prosecutors pledging to 'refrain from prosecuting those who seek, provide, or support abortions' (one of four Virginia CAs alongside Biberaj/Loudoun, Dehghani-Tafti/Arlington, Descano/Fairfax). Categorical pre-commitment to declination of any future VA abortion restrictions — clears bar for FALSE on both the public-profession cell and the affirmative-CA-action cell. HARD adverse public_justice q0 evidence: Morales indicted Officer Stephen Rankin and secured a manslaughter conviction in 2016 for the William Chapman II killing — she told the jury Rankin 'brought a gun into what is at worst a fist fight'; also indicted Officer Jeremy Durocher. She is 'the only prosecutor in the country to have successfully indicted more than 1 officer involved in an on-duty shooting' (Real Justice PAC endorsement page). The Portsmouth police union sued her; she has made no anti-defund quote and no affirmative pro-police-staffing advocacy — the rubric's HIGH BAR is not met; this is the inverse pattern. HARD adverse public_justice q1 evidence: Platform explicitly advocates 'appropriate reforms of cash bail to ensure that non-violent offenders are not being detained pre-trial based solely on inability to pay'; she co-signed a national prosecutors' letter to NY Gov. Cuomo urging bail reform; called on the Virginia General Assembly to 'end mandatory sentencing minimums, cash bail, and the death penalty.' Affirmative cash-bail-reform advocacy — adverse to the rubric's pretrial-detention cell. HARD adverse public_justice q3 evidence: Platform commits to 'lower the overall rate of incarceration in favor of other means of achieving justice and safety' and 'materially reducing prosecution of nonviolent minor behavioral offenses' — explicit public-health/decarceral framing the rubric scores against. HARD adverse self_defense q0 evidence: Morales has publicly explained and supported Virginia's red-flag (Extreme Risk Protective Order) law in WAVY/MSN video segments and is vocally pro-prosecution on gun violence — she requested AG Miyares's help launching Operation Ceasefire in Portsmouth in 2023. Active support for restrictive firearms enforcement; categorical opposition to the local 2A protection cell. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on biblical_marriage (any cell — no public statement on natural marriage, biological sex/trans policy, or Pride proclamations / LGBTQ-rally appearances in her CA capacity), family_child_sovereignty (any cell — out of CA's lane), christian_liberty (any cell — no church affiliation; generic 'free association and peaceful expression of dissenting views' platform line is First Amendment boilerplate, not Christian-liberty specific), economic_stewardship (out of CA's lane; no BBA framing), election_integrity (any cell), public_justice q2 (restorative-justice diversion offered but no restitution-first framing) and q4 (no Romans-13 framing), refuse_state_overreach (any cell — she met cooperatively with AG Miyares on Operation Ceasefire 2023 rather than refusing state coordination — neutral, not adverse, not affirmative), border_immigration q1/q2 (no explicit ICE-cooperation policy located; Portsmouth was on the May 2025 DHS sanctuary list and Portsmouth Sheriff [separate office] cancelled a Warrant Service Officer agreement with ICE — but no direct Morales quote located; her FJP coalition membership is soft adverse signal but not a scoreable cell — left null), self_defense q1 (no firearms-in-buildings or CCW-restriction policy located beyond her general red-flag-law support which q0 already captures; left null to avoid double-counting). Counter-archetype context: NONE — textbook FJP / Real Justice PAC / Local Progress progressive prosecutor. Her one establishment-cooperative move (meeting AG Miyares 2023 on Operation Ceasefire) was framed as collaboration, not a policy reversal.

Cosponsored H.R. 8404, the Respect for Marriage Act of 2022, which repealed DOMA and codified federal recognition of same-sex and interracial marriages — explicitly rejecting the one-man-one-woman definition of marriage the rubric affirms as the constitutional ideal.

Was the lone Democrat to vote against repealing the 2002 Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq on June 17, 2021 — the chamber voted 268–161 and Democrats favored repeal 219–1, with only Luria dissenting — placing her squarely against the rubric's call to end forever wars and repeal open-ended congressional war authorizations.

Running as a Democrat for Virginia's 2nd Congressional District in the 2026 cycle, Mosolf is campaigning in a state where the Virginia General Assembly placed the Right to Reproductive Freedom Amendment on the November 2026 ballot — a measure supported by every Democrat in the legislature and opposed by every Republican. Mosolf's platform of 'restoring America's democracy' and protecting healthcare access — consistent with the Virginia Democratic Party's unified support for constitutional abortion rights — places him on record against a life-at-conception or personhood standard. His former USAID career overseeing local development programs in Lebanon, Gaza-adjacent, and other fragile states is also consistent with support for reproductive-health services internationally.

Opposes 'irrational tariffs' as harmful to Americans and campaigns for expanding Affordable Care Act subsidies, opening new public-option healthcare pathways, and more aggressively regulating the insurance and pharmaceutical industry — a government-expansion posture that conflicts with the rubric's balanced-budget and anti-deficit standard.

A former senior USAID official married to a naturalized immigrant, Mosolf has not called for mandatory deportation or mass removal of those in the country unlawfully; his platform centers on restoring democratic governance and legal immigration pathways rather than the deportation-first enforcement the rubric favors.

claims.text: Self-described 'proud Marine' and 'devout man of faith,' Gray explicitly states 'Boys don't belong in girls' sports' and pledges to fight to pass Sage's Law — Virginia's 2023 statute requiring school notification to parents before socially transitioning a minor — making protection of children from gender ideology a cornerstone of his campaign against incumbent Eugene Vindman.

Gray campaigns on defending parental rights, supporting school choice, and insisting that 'public schools should educate and not indoctrinate students' — a broad parental-sovereignty platform that encompasses school vouchers, curriculum transparency, and opposition to government overreach in children's upbringing.

Signed the U.S. Term Limits pledge committing to co-sponsor and vote for a constitutional amendment to term limit members of Congress — rejecting career-politician incumbency and limiting federal entrenchment of power.

As the 2019 Republican nominee for Prince William County Board Chair, Gray's campaign platform explicitly committed to maintaining the removal of criminal illegal aliens under the 287(g) program — calling for him to 'Maintain the removal of criminal illegal aliens under Corey Stewart's highly successful 287(g) program' — and to prevent Prince William from 'becoming a sanctuary county.' These positions are directly continuous with his 2026 congressional campaign for VA-07, which is organized around the America First Agenda including enforcement-first immigration policy and opposition to sanctuary designations for local jurisdictions.

Gray's 2026 congressional campaign website describes him as a 'devout man of faith' who raised his children on 'the core values of God, hard work, and patriotism,' and emphasizes that his USMC service reinforced his commitment to 'defending this nation's values' — including the value of faith in public and civic life. Running on the America First Agenda with a campaign identity rooted explicitly in his Christian faith, Gray's platform aligns with the principle of vigorous defense of religious free exercise and the protection of faith communities from government encroachment in public life.

claims.text: Declared herself 'proudly pro-life' and publicly cheered the Supreme Court's 2022 Dobbs decision overturning Roe v. Wade, opposing any abortion access as incompatible with the value she places on unborn life from conception.

Stated 'our right to keep and bear arms must never be infringed' and, as Prince William County Supervisor, actively fought against gun-control resolutions — opposing the regulatory framework the rubric's self-defense plank targets.

During her 2022 and 2024 campaigns for Virginia's 7th Congressional District, Vega made the migrant crisis at the southern border one of her top campaign priorities, appearing on Fox News to criticize Biden-era border policy failures and calling for security and rule of law at the border — positions consistent with wall construction, military border enforcement, and ending catch-and-release. Ballotpedia confirms border security was among her primary campaign platforms. Her law-enforcement career (patrol officer, hostage negotiator) reinforces her emphasis on strong deterrence, placing her in alignment with the rubric's border_immigration[0] (wall and military deployment at the border).

In her 2022 congressional questionnaire, Vega declared strong opposition to H.R.1 ('For the People Act'), stating 'I strongly oppose bills like HR1 which would allow the likes of Nancy Pelosi and AOC to dictate the way elections in Virginia are run' — affirming the rubric's demand for state-controlled, voter-ID-backed election integrity rather than federalized, ID-free voting rules.

Stated in her 2022 voter-guide questionnaire that 'the government should cut spending in order to reduce the national debt,' running on a platform of 'limited government' and opposing wasteful federal spending. Her campaign framing consistently tied inflation and cost-of-living pain to excess government expenditure — aligning with the rubric's anti-deficit fiscal discipline standard.

At a 2022 candidate forum, Vega stated: 'As a Christian, I support the traditional definition of marriage.' As a Prince William County Supervisor, she voted NO on amending county code to comply with Virginia's Values Act (which extended non-discrimination protections to LGBTQ residents) in June 2020, was one of only two supervisors to oppose the measure in a 5-2 board vote, and declined to support the board's 2020, 2021, and 2022 LGBTQ Pride Month proclamations — a consistent record of opposing government recognition of same-sex relationships and LGBTQ identity as protected legal categories.

Vega made parental rights over children's education and identity a central 2022 campaign theme. Co-campaigning with Tulsi Gabbard on an explicit parental-rights platform, she declared: 'I'm sick and tired of a government that is telling me how to lead my life, how to spend my money, and most importantly, how to raise my children!' She supported Gov. Youngkin's policy requiring parental notification when schools discuss children's gender identity, stating it 'gives parents in the know as to what is happening with their children.' She also publicly opposed CRT in public schools and stated 'Education doesn't start at school, it starts at home,' urging parents to advocate at school board meetings.

Appearing on the John Fredericks Show during the 2022 campaign, Vega stated that U.S. assistance to Ukraine 'has to end' — one of the clearest anti-Ukraine-aid positions taken by a Virginia congressional candidate that cycle. She co-campaigned with Tulsi Gabbard, an outspoken critic of U.S. military aid to Ukraine and of NATO expansion. Vega also used general anti-foreign-spending framing: 'We keep printing money that we don't have and sending money to all of these places when we have a great need here,' framing overseas commitments as incompatible with domestic priorities.

claims.text: Supports a 15-week abortion ban with exceptions for rape, incest, and the life of the mother, and has stated he opposes prosecuting women who seek abortions—a restrictions-only position that falls short of the rubric's life-from-conception/personhood standard and the abolitionist 'no exceptions' call.

As Virginia's Attorney General (2022–2026), continued actively defending Virginia's Universal Background Check law and One Handgun a Month statute in court after the Bruen ruling made such laws constitutionally suspect; declined to join 26 other Republican AGs in an amicus brief against Washington State's magazine-capacity restrictions—drawing sustained criticism from Gun Owners of America as a 'Second Amendment betrayal.'

As Virginia Attorney General, Miyares repeatedly led or joined multistate religious-liberty defenses: in 2022 he supported religious exemptions for Navy SEALs from the COVID-19 vaccine mandate (coalition of 21 AGs), urged the Supreme Court to uphold Coach Kennedy's right to pray on the football field, and fought transit-authority religious viewpoint discrimination; in 2023 he led 19 Republican AGs in demanding the FBI explain its alleged profiling of traditionally observant Catholics. In 2025, weeks before leaving office, he led a new multistate religious-liberty coalition defense — a consistent, years-long record matching the rubric's free-exercise standard.

Not on 2026 VA Senate ballot (R field is Farington/Mizusawa/Williams). Scores now evidence-based from 2024 Senate campaign statements; archetype all-TRUE cleared; null where no citable position.

claims.text: During his 2024 Virginia Senate campaign, stated 'Life begins at conception' and expressed being 'thrilled' by the Supreme Court's Dobbs ruling overturning Roe v. Wade — affirming a life-from-conception standard.

Campaign platform stated 'The Constitution is clear regarding the Second Amendment. I will always protect the right to self defense' and Cao strictly opposes new legislation he considers an infringement on Second Amendment rights.
Listed 'Securing our borders' as a core federal priority during his 2024 Senate campaign, calling border defense part of 'providing for the common defense' — the federal government's primary duty.

During his 2024 Virginia Senate campaign, Cao stated 'our economy is hurting because of reckless spending' and highlighted his experience reviewing and overseeing the Navy's $140 billion budget. He expressed a desire to serve on the Appropriations Committee, citing his ability to 'review line items and eliminate unnecessary spending' — consistent with the rubric's anti-deficit/balanced-budget standard.

Following Kennedy v. Bremerton School District (2022) — which protected a public school football coach's right to pray in view of students after games — Cao stated: 'The right to express one's faith is a cornerstone of this country. I'm glad to see religious freedom being protected by the Court.' His support for the ruling reflects his commitment to robust free exercise of religion in the public square.

During his 2024 Virginia Senate campaign, Cao stated that same-sex marriage is 'a states' rights issue' but that 'at the federal level, it needs to be illegal,' opposing federal codification of same-sex marriage. A Virginia Catholic Conference voter guide rated his position as 'mostly ban' on same-sex marriage, consistent with the rubric's one-man-one-woman standard.

Cao made election integrity a centerpiece of his 2024 Virginia Senate campaign, demanding photo voter ID and declaring 'We demand election integrity. We cannot have a democratic republic if we cannot trust the legitimacy of our elections.' He stated, 'If you can show an ID to go to a movie theater, you had better be required to show an ID to vote.'
